description_full:
We are Workerbase – building an AI platform that digitizes manual manufacturing processes for global brands like Porsche, Siemens, and Bosch. Demand is growing fast. We need a Product Marketing Manager who can translate what we build into messaging that moves markets – and who understands the world of industrial enterprise buyers.




If you know how to make complex technology feel obvious to a traditional enterprise audience – keep reading. 👇




⭐ WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE EXCITING




🏭 Real industry, real stakes. You're not marketing a SaaS tool to software teams. You're helping Bosch, Schaeffler, and Porsche rethink how work gets done on the shopfloor. Your messaging shapes how the world's largest manufacturers make buying decisions.

🧩 Positioning that actually matters. We're early in a category we're helping to define. You'll own how we show up to manufacturing and industrial enterprise buyers – a segment that demands credibility, precision, and proof.

🚀 Full-funnel ownership. From ICP definition and messaging to sales enablement, launch campaigns, and analyst relations – you set the strategy and execute it. No waiting for someone to hand you a brief.

🤖 AI as your operating system. You'll have full latitude to build AI-assisted workflows into how you research, create, and execute – not as an experiment, but as how we actually work. If you've been wanting to operate this way but your current setup won't let you, this is the role.

🏎️ Startup speed, enterprise context. No corporate layers. You'll work directly with founders, product, and sales to shape how Workerbase competes and wins.




🪄 WHAT YOU'LL DO




Own positioning and messaging for Workerbase – across industries, personas, and use cases – anchored in manufacturing and industrial enterprise.
Define and evolve our ICP. You know who we're targeting, why they buy, and what makes them hesitate.
Build and maintain the messaging architecture: value propositions, persona narratives, competitive differentiators.
Enable the sales team with battle cards, pitch decks, case study narratives, and objection-handling frameworks.
Plan and execute product launches – working closely with product to turn new features into market moments.
Drive content strategy for the enterprise buyer journey: thought leadership, customer stories, industry reports.
Develop analyst and influencer relationships in the manufacturing and industrial tech space.
Use AI tools to accelerate content creation, research, and campaign execution.




🤝 YOU'RE A FIT IF YOU




Have 5+ years of product marketing experience in B2B SaaS – ideally with exposure to manufacturing, industrial automation, ERP, MES, or other traditional enterprise verticals.
Know how to speak to enterprise buyers who are skeptical of hype and need to see ROI before they'll move.
Can take a technical platform and turn it into a crisp, compelling story – for a VP of Operations as easily as for a procurement lead.
Use AI tools daily to work faster and smarter – not as a novelty, but as part of how you actually get things done.
Are comfortable being hands-on: you write the copy, build the deck, run the launch – and you enjoy it.
Bonus: Speak German at C1 level or above.
Bonus: You've worked with or sold into manufacturing, logistics, or production environments.
Bonus: You've built a category narrative or repositioned a product in a crowded market.




🎁 WHAT YOU GET




Real ownership from day one – you shape the function, not just fill a role.
A seat at the table with founders, product, and sales leadership.
Flexible, hybrid work setup based in Munich.
Competitive salary and benefits.
Learning budget to keep growing.
Sports & wellness membership via Wellpass.
Stock options to share in our success.
A central Munich office and legendary company events.




🔎 ABOUT US




We exist because there's a smarter way to produce. With environmental and demographic challenges ahead, we're on a mission to future-proof the production operations of the world's largest manufacturers.




We patch the gap between frontline workers, machines, and data – digitizing manual work with AI in a low-code environment connected to the manufacturer's core IT systems. We believe technology should empower people, not replace them.




If you're ready to upend "we've always done it this way" – we want to meet you.

Open Role: Graduate Product Marketing Manager

Marketers at Canonical are brand stewards
description_full:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.

Open Role: Graduate Product Marketing Manager

Marketers at Canonical are brand stewards and demand creators.They transform complex open-source technology into messages, stories and strategies that shape the market understanding of our vision. Some focus on differentiated product and portfolio positioning; others specialize in account-based engagement, partner and alliance messaging, or product-led growth. Whatever their specialization, each marketer works as part of a cross-functional unit, contributing campaigns and insights that help the company learn and grow faster.

We have a bias for action, focus efforts on the most impactful activities and take ownership of our results. We work across long buying cycles, balance storytelling with data, design experiments, track outcomes, and iterate rapidly to find what works.

Marketers at Canonical don’t just promote open source; they amplify it, ensuring our innovations reach the right people, at the right time, with clarity and purpose from developers to decision makers.

As a Graduate PMM, you won't just be watching from the sidelines; you will be hands-on from day one, collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate complex technical innovations into compelling market value.

Location: This role will be based remotely in the EMEA region and will report to the Product Marketing Team Manager

What Your Day Will Look Like


GTM Support: Contribute to go-to-market planning and assist in the execution of product launches across various sectors.
Storytelling: Draft and edit compelling messaging and positioning that highlights business value for diverse target audiences.
Campaign Coordination: Assist in developing campaign plans and content dissemination strategies to ensure our message reaches the right people.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with demand generation and digital teams to help optimize marketing funnels and track campaign performance.
Event & Social Support: Work with the wider marketing team to coordinate webinars, events, and social media promotions.
Market Research: Monitor the competitive landscape and industry trends to help refine our product differentiation.



What We Are Looking For In You


Tech Curiosity: A genuine passion for technology and a desire to learn about open source.
Analytical Thinking: An ability to break down complex issues and translate them into clear, actionable recommendations.
Collaboration: Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships within a fast-paced, cross-functional environment.
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills; you enjoy storytelling and creating impactful content.
Proactive Mindset: A self-starter who can prioritize tasks effectively and meet deadlines in a dynamic setting.
Foundational Knowledge: A basic understanding of marketing principles or go-to-market strategies (via your degree, internships, or personal projects).
Growth Mindset: Someone who is eager to learn, open to feedback, and not afraid to take on new challenges.



What We Offer You

Your base pay will depend on various factors including your geographical location, level of experience, knowledge and skills. In addition to the benefits above, certain roles are also eligible for additional benefits and rewards including annual bonuses and sales incentives based on revenue or utilisation. Our compensation philosophy is to ensure equity right across our global workforce.

In addition to a competitive base pay, we provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. Please note that additional benefits may apply depending on the work location and, for more information on these, please ask your Talent Partner.


Fully remote working environment - we’ve been working remotely since 2004!
Personal learning and development budget of 2,000USD per annum
Annual compensation review
Recognition rewards
Annual holiday leave
Parental Leave
Employee Assistance Programme
Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues at ‘sprints’
Priority Pass for travel and travel upgrades for long haul company events



About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm that is at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do.

Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Work at Canonical is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game. Canonical provides a unique window into the world of 21st-century digital business.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

Own positi
description_full:
Sumsub is a leading full-cycle verification platform that enables scalable compliance. From identity and business verification to ongoing monitoring, our platform adapts to different risk appetites and market demands, ensuring global compliance. It allows customizing analytics and workflows with a no-code interface.

Over 4,000 clients — including Bitpanda, Wirex, Avis, Bybit, Vodafone, Duolingo, Kaizen Gaming, and TransferGo — trust Sumsub to accelerate growth, prevent fraud, and maintain compliance worldwide.

Drive adoption and revenue growth of the Reusable Identity product family (Reusable KYC and Sumsub ID) by defining strategic positioning, leading GTM initiatives, and turning deep customer insights into measurable impact across the full funnel.

What You Will Be Doing:


Own positioning, messaging, and narrative for Reusable Identity solutions (Reusable KYC and Sumsub ID), ensuring consistent communication across all customer touchpoints.
Lead go-to-market strategy and execution for both B2B and B2C audiences, including new product launches and scalable launch processes.
Drive growth and adoption by increasing Reusable KYC uptake among existing clients and accelerating direct end-user sign-ups for Sumsub ID.
Design and execute rapid experiments across acquisition, activation, and retention to unlock scalable growth channels and optimise performance.
Conduct continuous market and customer research to inform strategy, identify opportunities, and strengthen competitive positioning.
Plan and optimise multichannel marketing campaigns, collaborating closely with Brand & Creative to develop and test high-impact marketing assets (sales materials, landing pages, creatives).
Act as a hands-on connector between Product and GTM teams (Sales, Customer Success, Marketing), ensuring alignment on priorities, messaging, and execution.



About You:


5+ years of hands-on marketing or Product Marketing experience in SaaS, with direct experience working on B2C or B2C-style products.
Strong hands-on execution skills, with experience owning day-to-day Product Marketing work end to end — from research and messaging to launches, experiments, and asset creation.
Proven experience partnering closely with Product Managers, building strong working relationships and collaborating on roadmap priorities, user insights, and go-to-market execution.
Experience defining and launching products, including positioning, messaging, pricing and packaging input, and promotion strategies.
Solid experience in market research, customer segmentation, and translating insights into clear, actionable marketing decisions.
Experience planning, executing, and optimising complex, omnichannel marketing campaigns in fast-paced environments.
Strong analytical mindset, with the ability to use data and experimentation to drive decisions and continuous improvement.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with Product, Growth, Sales, Customer Success, and Creative teams.
Fluent written and spoken English.
Comfortable working independently in a high-pressure, competitive environment, demonstrating ownership, reliability, and a strong work ethic.



What We Offer:


Remote-first, trust-based culture. Work from the place that works best for you. No mandatory office days, no attendance trackers. In some locations, we provide offices or coworking spaces, but the choice is yours.
True flexibility. We do not fix you to a 9-to-5 schedule. You can adjust your working hours when needed, as long as your day stays productive and in sync with the team.
Extra time off. Your birthday is a holiday here. Add to that 10 personal days each year, seven sick days without paperwork, and extra time to enjoy Christmas and New Year. Time to rest is part of the deal.
Work that matters. Our mission is to build a digital world that is secure, accessible and inclusive for everyone. From fighting fraud to making online services easier and safer to use, your work will have a real impact on how people experience trust online.
Compensation. We offer fair and transparent pay, benchmarked to the market.
Truly global. We work across continents and time zones, with teammates and customers from all over the world. You will run campaigns that cross borders, cultures, and languages, and see your ideas land worldwide.
Growth built in. Clear goals, open feedback and personal development plans. We support your progress with learning opportunities and by covering role-specific events, from design conferences to marketing forums.
Team offsites. Sometimes just Slack is not enough. That is why we meet in person a few times a year. Trips are fully covered, so you can meet, collaborate, and recharge together.
Getting you set up. We make sure you have access to the tools and hardware you need to do your work well.
Friendly by design. Our logo is a dog for a reason. We keep things human, open and kind. We welcome individuality, quirks and different perspectives, because that is what makes our work smarter and more fun.



The hiring stages: TA screening -> Interview with Director of Product Marketing and Product Manager -> Assignment -> Final Interview with CMO.

Sounds like a great opportunity for your career development? Then go ahead and apply!

We are a global community of innovators, creators, and thinkers, and we believe that diversity fuels our innovation. Sumsub is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, genders, experiences, abilities and perspectives. Join us in shaping the future inclusively.

POSITION SUMMARY

As EMEA Product Marketing Manager, you will be part of the Vascular and Emergency Medicine (VEM) business unit. You will be responsible for the Arterial and Acute Haemodialysis ranges, which are part of the Central Access portfolio. Your role involves developing, implementing, and managing strategic marketing activities to maximize sales across the region. You will collaborate with and drive multi-disciplinary team activities involving country sales teams, support teams, and the Global Business Unit. You will report to the Senior Product Manager in charge of the Central Access portfolio.




PRINCIPAL RESPONSIBILITIES

- Investigate market opportunities for current and future products and evaluate market potential within the assigned ranges.

- Develop business and marketing plans, including market development, sales promotions, distribution, pricing, sales forecasts, and analysis of market trends.

- Oversee all list pricing in EMEA, monitoring actual ASPs (Average Selling Prices) and gross margins by country, and executing annual pricing policy amendments.

-Plan and coordinate new product launches, obsolescence programs, and product updates. Lead all communications, support materials, and deliverables to ensure successful market penetration.

- Partner with the Operational Marketing team to ensure strategic alignment on product lifecycle management, demand management, and supply chain logistics.

- Collaborate with the Communication and Digital teams to develop omnichannel marketing strategies and promotional assets.

- Monitor competitor activity within EMEA to develop effective positioning and competitive strategies for the sales force.

- Engage directly with customers and KOLs to identify unmet needs and ensure that marketing strategies are aligned with the clinical reality of the field.

- Maintain strong relationships with country sales teams, providing them with the necessary clinical tools, training, and feedback loops to achieve local targets.

- Review customer feedback and quality complaints, proactively relaying information to the appropriate departments with consistent follow-up.

- Attend international congresses to represent the product lines and gather regional market intelligence.




EDUCATION / EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS

- Experience: 1–2 years in Product Management or Marketing. Experience in Vascular Access is a plus.

- Education: Master’s degree level required.

- Computer Skills: Proficient in the Microsoft Office Suite. Experienced in using Salesforce and Power BI to analyse data and present outcomes effectively.

- Fluency in English is required.

- Organizational Skills: Ability to organize information quickly and multi-task to meet commitments; must work independently and accept accountability.

- Public Speaking: Strong presentation skills with the capacity to present effectively to large groups and clinical stakeholders




SPECIALIZED SKILLS & OTHER REQUIREMENTS

- Cross-Functional Management: Proven ability to lead and motivate multi-disciplinary teams.

- Communication: Ability to communicate company goals, strategic deadlines, and complex business hurdles effectively to the field.

description_full:
Heyflow is recognized as a TOP company by LinkedIn and Kununu (German Glassdoor). Join us NOW! 💙




Heyflow is the leading all-in-one lead generation platform in the market, combining every essential tool into a single, highly flexible no-code solution. Our diverse customer base includes Fortune 500 companies like Adecco Group, Cisco, and Credit Agricole, as well as fast growing scaleups such as RocketMortgage and 1KOMMA5°.




You'll be Heyflow's Senior Product Marketing Manager, the person who shapes how we position our product and brings it to market with real impact. In return, we'd love someone who's genuinely excited to roll up their sleeves.




What You'll Do

Own Heyflow’s product positioning by developing clear, compelling messaging that communicates our value to growth marketers, scaling teams, and enterprise buyers, based on a deep understanding of our product, customers, and competitive landscape.
Lead go-to-market launches for new features, working closely with Product Managers to translate what was built and why into impactful launch strategies and campaigns.
Execute end-to-end feature launches across channels including email, in-app messaging, landing pages, blog posts, and video, while tracking adoption and product usage after launch.
Develop and run product-driven growth initiatives, testing creative formats, angles, and channels to expand reach and drive adoption among both existing users and new audiences.
Collaborate closely with the CEO and Product teams, helping shape the product marketing function while independently designing and executing initiatives that move the business forward.
Act as a hands-on driver of impact, taking ideas from concept to execution and continuously optimizing how Heyflow is positioned and brought to market.




What You'll Bring

Experience in B2B SaaS product marketing, ideally in a growth-stage environment, with a strong understanding of positioning and go-to-market strategies.
High agency and entrepreneurial thinking, with a track record of building, driving, or figuring things out independently rather than waiting for detailed instructions.

Experience owning go-to-market launches end-to-end, from strategy through execution and measurement.

Why Heyflow?

Just like fingerprints, each of us is one-of-a-kind, and that's why we believe in catering to your individual needs. description_full:
Description

Legartis is on a mission: to unlock legal work at scale.

We have already set the standard for AI-powered contract review, helping teams work with greater precision than ever before. But that is just the beginning. We are building a holistic platform that goes far beyond review - connecting people, ideas, and knowledge to fundamentally change how legal teams operate.

When you join Legartis, you don’t just take a job - you take ownership. We are a team of product builders and legal experts who believe that true problem-solving requires more than just code; it requires a thoughtful process. We don't chase hype; we build substance. If you’re a tech savvy product marketer who understands the audience and fuels growth through product marketing, this is your opportunity to make a significant impact in an emerging industry.

Ready to build great things together?

Please submit your CV along with a Cover Letter outlining your Ambition to join Legartis. Applications without a Cover Letter will not be accepted.

Your mission

You shape how Legartis wins in the market:


Make our products clearly differentiated and highly competitive - by developing and continuously sharpening positioning and messaging across our portfolio.
Drive go-to-market strategy and execution for launches, expansion, and adoption - from plan to impact.
Find market opportunities others miss - deep knowledge of our ICP, competitive landscape, and buyer needs isn't just insight work, it's how you shape what we build and how we sell it.
Arm every team to win - create the assets, narratives, and enablement materials that make Sales, Marketing, and CS more effective.
Feed product strategy with market truth - bring customer and competitive intelligence back to the product team so we build what matters.
Work across the company, not in a silo - you sit in Product, but your influence reaches Marketing, Sales, and leadership.


Your profile


4+ years in marketing, growth, product marketing, or product management at a SaaS company. Creativity matters more than a traditional background.
Proven strategist: demonstrated success creating and executing marketing strategies.
Storyteller: you craft compelling materials about complex AI products for non-technical audiences.
Clear communicator: exceptional written and verbal communication with a strong visual sensibility.
Impact-driven: comfortable with experimentation and interpreting both quantitative and qualitative data.
AI-savvy: crafty with AI tools and automations — you use them daily and keep exploring what's new.
Fluent in English and German.
Nice to have: previous experience in legal tech.
You are warm, pragmatic, and naturally curious. You ask the right questions, communicate clearly, and have a bias toward action. You are a true team player who elevates the people around you.


Why us?


Shape the future of Legal Tech in an emerging market building an impactful AI product.
Opportunity to work self organised and add to technology as well as features.
Work with a talented and dedicated team of researchers and engineers. Here you can make an impact and grow professionally.
Competitive salary and participation of the Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP).
Modern office in Leipzig with at least 2 days per week in the office or hybrid in Berlin.
Regular team events in Zurich, Leipzig and offsite like restaurant testing, hiking and more.


Process

Our process is built on respect and transparency:


30 min. interview with People & Operations Manager (cultural fit): Have a chat to get an understanding of Legartis and the role you are interested in. We in turn want to get to know you and your expectations of your next challenge.
20 min. interview with Head of Product (professional fit): Understand the professional requirements for this position better, as well as your future tasks and team setup.
1h interview with Product and Marketing teams (case study): To demonstrate your abilities, you have an in-depth interview with our product team. You showcase your abilities by presenting a real life use case in product marketing.
Additional interviews: Meet the team you would be working with and take the opportunity to pose any remaining questions. One meeting will be in our Leipzig office to meet the team in person and get to know the work place.
Decision is made: If we’re considering each other to be the right fit, you are welcomed by the Legartis team!


About Us

We are Legartis, a LegalTech company that is changing the way contracts are reviewed and analyzed today. Our SaaS solution uses artificial intelligence to automate contract review for legal departments and law firms. We are a growing and ambitious team on a mission to eliminate repetitive tasks in everyday legal work. Since our founding in 2017, we have won various awards such as the Swiss Fintech Awards, been listed as a Top100 startup in Switzerland and named by Forbes as one of the best AI startups in the DACH region.

description_full:
About Dennemeyer X-Labs

Dennemeyer X‑Labs is the pioneering software arm of Dennemeyer, a global leader in Intellectual Property (IP) services. Our three strategic pillars—Disruptive Products, Innovative Platforms, and New Venture—drive the development of solutions such as our AI‑powered patent search platform (Octimine), the Simple IP management system for small corporates, the IP Lounge SaaS platform as a gateway to Dennemeyer services, and an internal GPT platform enabling efficiency gains through Agentic AI. We also develop and maintain the Dennemeyer API, enabling seamless integration of third‑party IP management systems with Dennemeyer’s extensive IP service offering. Additionally, we build strategic partnerships that strengthen our ecosystem and extend the reach of our technology.

About The Role

We are seeking a Product Marketing Manager who excels at translating customer insights into clear positioning and effective go‑to‑market strategies. In this role, you will collaborate closely the with the product teams of Simple IP, Octimine, and the Dennemeyer API and our central marketing unit to ensure consistent, impactful market communication.

You will define value propositions, develop targeted messaging, and drive launch and growth initiatives that strengthen our market presence. You bring a structured, market‑led perspective and help align our portfolio’s go‑to‑market execution with the company’s long‑term vision.

Your Tasks

Product-Specific Priorities:


Develop, own and execute the end-to-end Go-to-Market roadmap for Octimine, strengthening the product's strategic positioning while hands-on managing the launches for new features.
Support the GTM roadmap for our innovative platform, Simple IP, by quantitatively testing and validating product positioning and messaging to identify the optimal market fit.
Lead the strategic marketing roadmap for our APIs to drive growth through collaborative partner marketing, managing joint PR, interviews, and co-branded campaigns across both online and offline channels.



Core Responsibilities


Apply a data-first mindset to all initiatives. You will use A/B testing, market analytics, and performance metrics to validate positioning shifts and measure the success of marketing initiatives.
Own the development and maintenance of all marketing collateral from digital landing pages and sales decks to offline physical assets, ensuring technical specs are translated into compelling value propositions.
Act as the central nervous system between PMs, Marketing, and Sales. You ensure the sales team is fully enabled with the tools they need to win and that the product vision is clearly communicated to the market.
You are the primary owner of the product marketing lifecycle. We are looking for a self-starter who manages their own workflow and priorities without needing constant direction. You don't just support the process; you proactively identify what needs to move, then execute, iterate, and optimize across multiple product lines simultaneously.



Your Profile


You have experience in Product Marketing with a proven track record of managing product launches and GTM strategies in a technical environment.
You possess a strong quantitative approach and rely on metrics and A/B testing rather than assumptions to validate positioning and campaign success.
You are a natural self-starter who thrives on autonomy. You are comfortable managing your own priorities and workflow across multiple products without the need for constant oversight.
You can adapt your style to different product needs, whether you are leading the charge for a mature software, testing an innovative new platform, or managing partner relationships.
You have a talent for translating complex technical specs into compelling narratives that resonate with customers and empower sales teams.
You are an agile operator who is equally effective at high-level roadmap planning and hands-on asset creation for both online and offline channels.
Experience in B2B SaaS or Intellectual Property (IP) knowledge is considered a strong plus.



Join and grow with us!


Develop and customize your long-time career within a fast growing multinational company
Enjoy working in multicultural teams and contribute to a diverse and international working environment
Organize your lifestyle with flexible working hours
Take the opportunity to act independently and entrepreneurial in your area of responsibility and profit from flat hierarchies and short decision-making processes
Celebrate success together as one team at our various team and corporate events
Attend a tailor-made onboarding program as well as individual training & development opportunities
Contribute through your personality and your daily work to make an impact on our business, corporate culture, society and environment

description_full:
Wir sind ein weltweit aktives mittelständisches Unternehmen mit Hauptsitz in München und Hersteller von elektronischen Geräten für anspruchsvolle Industrieanwendungen. Mit wireless charging und Lithium-Ionen Produkten für die mobile Robotik Anwendung, erobern wir nun auch mit einer Business Unit in Freiburg den Internationalen Markt. Als eigenständige Unit ist Wiferion – a PULS Business Unit seit 2023 Teil der PULS GmbH. Mit Wiferion möchten wir nicht nur den Start-Up Spirit in Freiburg weiterleben lassen, sondern auch das Produkt mit mehr Mitteln und Möglichkeiten stetig weiter entwickeln. Die Business Unit Wiferion liefert Ihre innovativen Ladesysteme und Batterien mittlerweile in fast alle Region der Welt und versorgt mobile Roboter und Gabelstapler in Produktion und Intralogistik mit benötigter Energie.




Product Marketing Manager*




Ihre Aufgaben:

Damit kein Kunde an unseren Produkten vorbei kann, erstellen Sie spezifischen Content für unsere neue Produktreihe Wiferion für all unsere Kanäle – Ihrer Kreativität sind dabei keine Grenzen gesetzt!
Technisch immer up to date – mit Ihrem technischen Know How zu Wireless Charging unterstützen Sie auch die Marketing-Kollegen in anderen Anfragen.
In Ihrer Schnittstellenfunktion zwischen Produktmanagement und Marketing sammeln, strukturieren und aktualisieren Sie Informationen zu bestehenden Produkten, Neuprodukten und Launch Materialien – immer mit Blick auf eine klare, zielgruppengerechte Kommunikation.
Der Kunde ist König! - Also maximieren Sie die Customer Experience mit Ihrem innovativen Marketing-Content.
Um die Customer Experience langfristig auf ein neues Level zu heben, arbeiten Sie intensiv an unserer Customer Documentation Plattform mit und erweitern diese stetig.
Darüber hinaus wirken Sie an der Weiterentwicklung unserer E-Learning-Plattform mit und bereiten Inhalte für interne Kolleginnen und Kollegen sowie externe Kunden auf.
Sie wissen was die Kunden wollen! – Sie analysieren die Kundenbedürfnisse und passen Ihre Marketing Strategie optimal an diese an.




Unsere Wünsche:

Idealerweise bringen Sie ein abgeschlossenes Studium in Kommunikationsdesign, BWL mit Schwerpunkt Marketing, Mediendesign, Grafikdesign oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang mit. Entscheidend ist aber Ihre fundierte Berufserfahrung im Bereich Produkt Marketing – optimalerweise im technischen Produktmarketing.
Mithilfe von Adobe Illustrator, die Adobe Suite sowie die Microsoft‑Programme mit Schwerpunkt auf PowerPoint, zaubern Sie immer wieder kreativen Content für all unsere Kanäle.
Ihre Hands-on Mentalität sowie Ihr ausgeprägtes technisches Interesse, Ihre kreative Ader und eine hohe technische Affinität, machen Sie unverzichtbar für unser Team.
Im Zentrum Ihres Tuns steht stets der Kunde neben unserem innovativen Produkt – dies kennen Sie auch bisher aus vergangenen Tätigkeiten.
Kommunikation und Zusammenarbeit in multinationalen Teams als auch mit externen Partnern sind uns sehr wichtig. Fachliche Themen stimmen Sie deshalb in verhandlungssicherem Deutsch und Englisch ganz selbstverständlich und souverän ab.







Sie sind uns wichtig!

Willkommen in unserem Team! Hier erwarten Sie nicht nur spannende berufliche Herausforderungen, sondern auch eine Unternehmenskultur, die auf Offenheit und Teamgeist basiert. Bei uns erleben Sie flache Hierarchien, die Raum für Ihre Ideen und Kreativität schaffen. Zudem bieten wir diverse Benefits, wie Urlaubsgeld, eine Zusatzversicherung, Homeoffice-Möglichkeiten und einen sicheren Arbeitsplatz.




In unserem Team erwartet Sie nicht nur anspruchsvolle Arbeit, sondern auch eine Vielfalt an Aufgaben, die Abwechslung in Ihren Arbeitsalltag bringen. Und für die nötige Energie sorgt unsere hochwertige Siebträger-Maschine, die besten Kaffee für Ihre Pausen zaubert – denn bei uns lieben wir guten Kaffee genauso wie innovative Technologien.




Als Teil eines erfolgreichen internationalen Unternehmens arbeiten Sie nicht nur mit tollen Kollegen zusammen, sondern auch an einer innovativen Technologie. Hier haben Sie die Möglichkeit, sich aktiv einzubringen und Teil eines Teams zu sein, das gemeinsam die Zukunft gestaltet.




Über Ihre Bewerbung freut sich Sandra Mayer. Gerne beantworten wir auch persönlich unter der Telefonnummer 089 9278 327 Ihre Fragen.




*Alle sind bei der PULS willkommen. Dieses Stellenangebot bezieht sich daher auf Alle, die gerne Teil unseres Unternehmens werden möchten. Unabhängig von kultureller & ethnischer Herkunft, Geschlecht, Religion, Weltanschauung, körperlicher Einschränkung oder Alter.

description_full:
About Us

HBX Group is a global technology‑driven travel organisation, connecting and empowering the world of travel through innovative solutions. We combine technology, data, and exceptional talent to deliver value to partners worldwide, always operating with a global approach, local touch mindset.




Job Summary:

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail‑oriented Strategy Analyst to support the Strategic Performance Lead and the Head of Strategic Performance. This role is critical in helping develop and maintain internal factbases, monitor progress of key strategic initiatives (“Big Bets”), and deliver high‑quality reporting and insights for senior leadership. The Analyst plays a key enabling role in the function, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and alignment across all strategic performance activities.




Responsibilities:

Support the Strategic Performance Lead in preparing materials and analysis for the weekly Big Bets forum and other executive‑level meetings.
Build and maintain internal factbases by gathering, validating, and synthesising data from multiple sources.
Conduct performance tracking for all Big Bets, monitoring progress, risks, blockers, and mitigation plans.
Prepare reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior management, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and alignment with strategic priorities.
Assist in the implementation and sharing of best practices across the function and with other teams.
Coordinate with cross‑functional teams (e.g., Finance, Transformation, Strategy Business Partners) to gather input and ensure alignment.
Support ad‑hoc deep dives, strategic reviews, and special projects as requested by the Strategic Performance Lead or Head of Strategic Performance.




Skillset

Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and data interpretation skills.
Experience preparing reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior leadership.
Ability to work with large datasets, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and quality.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to synthesise complex information clearly.
Strong collaboration skills and experience working cross‑functionally.
Highly organised, detail‑oriented, and able to manage multiple workstreams simultaneously.
Proactive mindset, with the ability to support strategic initiatives and adapt to evolving priorities.
Experience in strategy, business performance, transformation, or a similar analytical role is an advantage.




Experience Required

1–3 years of experience in strategy, business analysis, project management, or a similar analytical role (internships included).
Experience in data analysis, research, or project support is highly valued.
Exposure to working in a global or matrixed organization is a plus.




At HBX Group, we believe that diversity drives innovation and makes travel a force for good.

We're committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected, embracing different backgrounds, perspectives, and talents. Join us and be part of a team where diversity and equal opportunities really do make a difference

description_full:
Company Description

Sobi offers the opportunity to work at an international pharmaceutical company focused on specialty pharmaceuticals meeting the high medical needs of rare disease patients and providing treatment and services to them. Our employees come from a variety of backgrounds within research, healthcare, industry and the academic sphere.

Job Description

The Global Omnichannel Customer Engagement Lead will be responsible for delivering seamless and engaging experiences for healthcare professionals (HCPs) and other key stakeholders across all platforms, accelerating brand engagement and topline growth. This role is Global (Ex US) with a focus on EU and International Regions. The successful candidate will work in close collaboration with global cross functional and affiliates teams. This is a hands-on role requiring deep digital and omnichannel experience within the pharmaceutical industry. You should be confident to work independently in a matrix management environment, developing impactful customer journeys, high quality content and engaging affiliates in effective deployment across channels. Experience in rep triggered and blast emails, webinar platforms, HCPs portals, content tagging, Veeva and KAM orchestration are extremely important. The ability to communicate to stakeholders at all levels with clarity and vision is a must. Sobi is a dynamic environment which requires individuals that can work at pace and scale. This role provides tremendous opportunity to shape the digital and omnichannel roadmap for Sobi.

Key Responsibilities


Aligned to brand strategy, develop omnichannel plans, and advise on approach to campaign execution across markets
Develop and roll out best practice guidance to affiliates to deploy and execute campaigns effectively driving impact for the brands
Collaborate with cross functional teams (both commercial and non-commercial) to create personalized journeys based on customer preference data and brand objectives with simple and impactful KPIs
Develop omnichannel guidance and governance through playbooks, training and resources to support the business to execute in a consistent and impactful manner
Be seen as an SME championing strong customer engagement though omnichannel strategies
Build digital content ensuring consistent messaging, visuals and positioning across all channels and platforms
Support brand teams with their communication and messaging goals across non personal channels
Enhance and evolve Sobi’s HCP portal (SobiPro), incorporating industry best practices
Co-create channel capability with global and affiliate colleagues, providing strategic guidance
Set clear KPI’s and monitor campaigns to track performance and suggest enhancements
Support line manager to develop omnichannel and digital roadmap for the region(s)
Proactively assess future trends in customer engagement to accelerate our capability and provide Sobi with a competitive edge
Operate with high ethical standards in line with the Sobi corporate values and SOPs by ensuring ongoing completion of all required initial and ongoing training in local regulations and policies, and adherence to those regulations and policies


This is a highly networked role, with a requirement to work cross-functionally and cross-company.

Qualifications


University degree level education –Marketing, Engineering, Data or Computer Science
Digital/omnichannel experience at regional or global, including leading extensive digital projects within the pharmaceutical industry
Additional commercial experiences including sales, marketing and business intelligence
Experience in Cardiometabolic disorders is desirable
Proficient with CRM ecosystems such as Veeva
Data driven with strong data and analytics skills, Qlick/Power BI
Desirable experience in data flows/warehouse
Experienced in marketing automation across different customer segments
Ability to communicate complex digital strategies in simple, compelling narratives
Cross-cultural leadership, effective communication and team working skills
Multi stakeholder project management and leadership experience, delivering results on time and within budget
Flexibility, ability to manage change and operate in an environment of uncertainty
Fluency in written and spoken English
Additional European language skill is desirable


Additional Information

Please note:

As this role carries global responsibilities and can be based across Europe, it is published in several countries. You only need to submit one application — there is no need to apply separately to each posting.

description_full:
Hey there!

We’re Fever, the world’s leading tech platform for culture and live entertainment.

Our mission? To democratize access to culture and entertainment. With our proprietary cutting-edge technology and data-driven approach, we’re revolutionizing the way people engage with live entertainment.

Every month, our platform inspires over 300 million people in +55 countries (and counting) to discover unforgettable experiences while also empowering event creators with our data and technology, helping them scale, innovate, and enhance their events to reach new audiences.

Our results? We’ve teamed up with major industry leaders like Netflix, F.C. Barcelona, and Primavera Sound, presented international award-winning experiences, and are backed by several leading global investors! Impressive, right?

To achieve our mission, we are looking for bar-raisers with a hands-on mindset who are eager to help shape the future of entertainment!

Ready to be part of the experience?

Now, let’s discuss this role and what you will do to help achieve Fever’s mission.

Meet the Team

The Marketing & Growth team is one of the largest and most dynamic at Fever, playing a central role in driving visibility, engagement, and business impact across the company. We work closely with nearly every department, combining strategy, creativity, and data to connect millions of people with unforgettable experiences.

Join us if you want to drive global impact, master data-driven creativity, and help millions of people discover their next unforgettable experience.

The Role

As a Growth Analyst, you will independently lead high-impact global projects and strategies to drive Fever's entertainment market expansion across up to 40 countries. By designing end-to-end solutions and managing new business streams, you will collaborate across departments to enhance overall company performance.

You will support process optimization, coordinate with internal teams and external partners, and prepare insightful reports and presentations to steer strategic initiatives. We value individuals who work independently, take initiative, and excel as genuine team players—those who tackle tasks proactively, quickly adapt to new topics, and contribute innovative ideas. If you’re excited to shape a dynamic environment and make a tangible impact on our growth, you’re the perfect fit for our team.

Strategic Project Delivery Independently lead initiatives from conception to implementation. You will own the full project, managing timelines, resources, and risks ensuring goals translate into tangible results.
Process Optimization & Efficiency Analyze workflows across the organization to identify bottlenecks and implement scalable solutions. You will act as an internal consultant, helping departments operationalize their goals and reduce administrative friction.
Cross-Functional Bridge the gap between teams to ensure seamless information flow and collaboration. You will coordinate with internal stakeholders and external partners to maintain alignment in different projects.
Insights & Reporting Translate complex data into actionable insights. You will prepare high-quality reports, presentations, and KPI dashboards to support decision-making.




Qualifications:

We are not looking for perfect resumes, but for people who work independently, take responsibility, and are genuine team players.
You proactively tackle tasks, find solutions rather than problems, and can quickly adapt to new topics. At the same time, you value interaction with others, enjoy working cross-departmentally, and contribute ideas that propel the entire team forward.
If you are excited to get involved in a dynamic environment and truly make a difference, then you are exactly right for us!

description_full:
Airbnb was born in 2007 when two hosts welcomed three guests to their San Francisco home, and has since grown to over 5 million hosts who have welcomed over 2 billion guest arrivals in almost every country across the globe. Every day, hosts offer unique stays and experiences that make it possible for guests to connect with communities in a more authentic way.

The Community You Will Join

Our goal is to accelerate EMEA growth by enabling country teams to execute local growth plans — with a primary focus on driving demand. We work closely with marketing, growth marketing, product, communications, data science, and policy at both central and local level to develop scalable, holistic programs across markets.

The Difference You Will Make

As a Senior Growth & Operations Manager, EMEA, you will work with both Country and EMEA leadership to develop and execute growth initiatives to accelerate Airbnb’s business in the region.

Your attention to detail, analytical mindset, organization and communication skills will all ensure you succeed. You love to work on multiple priorities, run complex workstreams in parallel and thrive in fast-paced, ever changing environments. You want a role where you will both define strategies then flawlessly execute them. You're equally comfortable presenting to leadership and managing the operational details of a project.

This role is based in either London, Berlin, or Barcelona (hybrid, 2 days a week in the office), and is not eligible for relocation support.

A Typical Day


Own the conception, execution, and measurement of cross-functional business initiatives end-to-end. Concretely, this means defining a crisp problem statement, articulating the ideal solution to that problem, identifying key dependencies blocking that solution, working with cross-functional stakeholders to achieve the solution and driving post-mortem on learnings. These initiatives will span across the demand, supply, and regulatory surfaces of the business, both on tactical short-terms wins and strategic long-term actions.
Support business growth and acceleration by sourcing and operating innovative levers. Concretely, this means sizing, coordinating and operating pilots around broad topics such as traffic, CRM, growth marketing, new supply segments, distribution partnerships…
Drive regular business operation meetings and initiate meaningful conversations. Concretely, this means organizing and leading weekly meetings or working routines with cross-functional project teams (sales, marketing, product, community, legal…) to ensure key initiatives are on track and escalate accordingly when relevant.
Present analysis, insights and actionable plans to the regional leadership team to drive key business decisions. Concretely, this means using and merging available data sources and tools, internal and external to analyze macro and micro trends and define a key problem to solve. Then based on consultation with internal stakeholders, framing a recommendation and plan to support leadership decisions.


Your Expertise


Qualified candidates will have 6+ years of work experience, preferably with experience in business consulting or Strategy and Operations in a scale-up. Tech background is a plus.
Business background with understanding of operations and/or sales, growth marketing and commercial partnerships.
Strong project management experience working cross-functionally with multiple departments, ideally in tech companies or in innovation business units. Mastering of project management frameworks and methodologies. Ability to frame issues and develop hypotheses to solve them. Ability to organize and prioritize work, for oneself as well as for various functions to achieve common goals.
Analytical mindset, able to conduct end-to-end business analysis, leveraging excel and AI tools. SQL or Python knowledge is a plus.
Hands-on approach and detail-oriented. Getting things done fast and learning by doer mindset.
Written and verbal communicator with strong presentation and influence skills.
Fluent in English required; another European language is a strong plus.
Location: London, Berlin or Barcelona, willing to come frequently to the office.


Our Commitment To Inclusion & Belonging

Airbnb is committed to working with the broadest talent pool possible. We believe diverse ideas foster innovation and engagement, and allow us to attract creatively-led people, and to develop the best products, services and solutions. All qualified individuals are encouraged to apply.

How We'll Take Care Of You

Our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay is dependent upon many factors, such as: training, transferable skills, work experience, business needs and market demands. The base pay range shown below is annualized, is subject to change and may be modified in the future. This role may also be eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits.

Spain Annual Pay Range

€65.000—€80.000 EUR

description_full:
Founded in 2017, refurbed is Europe’s fastest-growing marketplace for refurbished products, active in 12 European countries and having surpassed €2Bn in GMV — all while being profitable. With beautiful headquarters in Vienna, we operate as a remote-first company, and our 250+ employees enjoy up to two months of workation per year. We’ve also been recognised as a Top DACH Employer by Kununu for three consecutive years.




Our mission is to make sustainable consumption the easy choice by enabling customers to buy products up to 40% cheaper while significantly reducing CO₂ emissions.




If you thrive in an environment that values momentum, ownership, and impact, you’ll feel at home here. We’re a fast-paced, high-performance team that works hard and challenges itself everyday. To enable this high-performance every team member enjoys full autonomy over their location (we’re remote-first).




The Category and Sales Marketing Analyst will play a crucial role in supporting vouchers and promotion operations, multi-category growth, improving supply-demand alignment, and strengthening the commercial foundations needed for successful marketing strategies across multiple categories, markets, and performance channels.




This role will directly enable Refurbed’s expansion across categories, brands and countries by taking ownership of operational and analytical tasks.




You will work closely with Demand Planning, Category Managers, CRM, Performance Marketing (Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates), Product, and Commercial teams.




WHO YOU ARE
2–5 years experience in e-commerce, category management, marketing operations, performance marketing, or commercial strategy.
Strong analytical and data-handling skills; able to create clear insights from complex data.
Highly organized, reliable, and operationally strong.
Comfortable coordinating with multiple teams and managing parallel workstreams across categories, markets, and performance channels.
Proactive mindset with strong problem-solving skills.
Experience with consumer electronics / SDA is a plus.
Experience with promotions or marketplace operations preferred.
Bonus: performance marketing fundamentals (e.g., Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates)




Location & eligibility: This role is open to candidates based in Europe (including UK) only with a valid right to work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You drive multi-category growth. You support prioritization and budget allocation across categories and markets, turning demand-side insights (search, click data, pricing signals) into clear, actionable growth opportunities and structured recommendations.
You run promotions end-to-end. You execute voucher campaigns across markets, monitor performance and ROI, and continuously optimize based on supply relevance, cost efficiency, and commercial impact.
You connect Marketing and Supply. You align teams by consolidating forecasts, stock, pricing, and partner inputs, ensuring everyone works off the same priorities and timelines while contributing clear insights to business reviews.
You enable brands and partners. You maintain key KPIs, ensure data readiness for activation, and support smooth rollout of brand campaigns.
You scale how we work. You build tools, dashboards, and documentation that enable teams to be more self-serve, while identifying automation opportunities to reduce manual effort.
You support performance execution. You help translate category priorities into performance marketing channels (Search, Paid Social, Affiliates) and ensure campaigns are set up for success.




WHY YOU WILL ENJOY WORKING WITH US:

Our Culture and Values:

Decisions should be made based on facts and not by hierarchy levels? We sure think so!
We embrace giving and receiving feedback, testing and welcoming new ideas, as well as sharing our knowledge across the company.
You fail sometimes? Well, so do we! We’re all just human, let’s learn from our mistakes to improve in the future!
No matter who you love, where you’re from, who you pray to, whether you pee sitting down or standing up – we are a bunch of talented people who enjoy spending our time making a difference!

Personal & Professional Development:

You'll have access to a dedicated learning budget within your department to support your growth, whether it's courses, conferences, or other learning opportunities that matter to you.
We partner with Likeminded, offering you private sessions with experts for your personal growth and mental health.
We frequently host internal workshops, allowing our teams to further develop their skills in different areas and enhancing collaboration across departments.

Flexibility:

We care about a healthy work/life balance. We not only function and support a fully remote setting but also offer the possibility to work from our beautiful office in the vibrant heart of Vienna – we love dogs, so feel free to bring your furry little buddy too 😊
We trust you to do the best job, so we don't care so much when you do it – you are able to self-organize.
You will also be invited to take part in our office weeks, happening three times a year, where you will have the chance to work side by side with your colleagues and enjoy the team-building events and on-site workshops




…and much more! See https://careers.refurbed.com/ for further insights!

description_full:
Vollgas nicht nur auf der Straße – sondern auch mit uns im Job!

Die Stellantis Germany GmbH ist Teil des weltweiten Automobilkonzerns Stellantis N.V.

In Deutschland ist die Stellantis Germany GmbH aus den beiden Unternehmensteilen

FCA Germany GmbH und Groupe PSA Deutschland GmbH hervorgegangen. Unter dem Dach von Stellantis werden die Marken Abarth, Alfa Romeo, Citroën, DS Automobiles, Fiat, Fiat Professional (leichte Nutzfahrzeuge), Jeep®, Leapmotor, Opel und Peugeot vertrieben.




Der Market Funnel Analyst (m/w/d) unterstützt die tägliche Umsetzung des Monitorings, der Diagnose und der Optimierung der Funnel-Performance.

Die Rolle nutzt regionale Dashboards, KPIs und Measurement-Frameworks und wendet zugleich marktspezifisches Know-how an, um Daten korrekt zu interpretieren und umsetzbare Optimierungsmaßnahmen zu empfehlen.

Diese Rolle stellt sicher, dass das regionale Reporting im Markt konsistent genutzt wird, und reichert es durch lokale Insights, Nuancen und Performance-Learnings an.




1. Funnel-Monitoring mit regionalen Dashboards

Performance über alle Funnel-Stufen hinweg mithilfe regionaler Tools tracken.
Anomalien, Trends, Wachstumschancen und Drop-offs identifizieren.
Performance anhand lokaler Channel-, Campaign- und Zielgruppen-Insights kontextualisieren.




2. Insight-Entwicklung für lokale Governance & regionale Abstimmung

Funnel-Insight-Summaries für Markt-Performance-Routinen vorbereiten.
Klare, kontextualisierte Erklärungen zu KPI-Bewegungen liefern.
Lokale Insights zu regionalen Reports und Playbooks beitragen.




3. Umsetzung von Optimierungen

Die Implementierung von Optimierungsmaßnahmen über Media, CRM und Content hinweg unterstützen.
A/B-Tests gemäß regionalen Frameworks durchführen, angepasst an Marktspezifika.
Test-Performance tracken und Learnings dokumentieren.




4. Reporting-Disziplin & Datenqualität

Sicherstellen, dass der Markt auf regionale Dashboards setzt (kein Parallel-Reporting).
Datenqualität validieren und Issues an regionale und lokale Owner eskalieren.
Konsistente Anwendung regionaler KPI-Definitionen sicherstellen.




5. Zusammenarbeit & Support

Eng mit CRM-, Media-, Market- und Brand-Teams zusammenarbeiten, damit Funnel-KPIs Ausführungsentscheidungen steuern.
Vorbereitung von Markt- und regionalen Performance-Reviews unterstützen.
Bei KPI-Schwankungen Deep-Dive-Diagnosen bereitstellen.




Unterstützte zentrale Funnel-KPIs

Awareness: Reichweite, CPR, Traffic-Qualität
Consideration: CTR, Engagement, Lead-Volumen, CPL
Intent & Conversion: Lead-to-Sale-Conversion, CPA
Retention: Loyalty-Rate, CRM-Lifecycle-KPIs
Effizienz: Effizienz der Budgetallokation
Forecasting: Prognosegenauigkeit (Demand/Leads/Sales)




Skills & Kompetenzen

Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten und hohe Detailgenauigkeit
Fähigkeit, Funnel-KPIs und Performance-Signale zu interpretieren
Sicherer Umgang mit Analytics-Plattformen (PowerBI, GA4, CRM-Tools)
Klare Kommunikation und Storytelling
Sicheres Arbeiten in strukturierten Frameworks
Problemlösungsorientiertes Mindset




Erfahrung

1–4 Jahre Erfahrung in Digital Analytics, Media Performance, CRM-Analytics oder Marketing Operations
Vorherige Erfahrung mit Funnel-KPIs wünschenswert
Erfahrung im Umgang mit standardisierten Reporting-Umgebungen







Interesse geweckt? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Sie erwartet eine attraktive und herausfordernde Tätigkeit eines dynamischen und internationalen Automobilkonzerns. Wenn Sie sich durch unsere Stellenausschreibung angesprochen fühlen, dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung (Motivationsschreiben, Lebenslauf, Zeugnisse) unter Angabe Ihres frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins und Gehaltswunsches.




Bei Stellantis beurteilen wir Kandidaten auf der Grundlage ihrer Qualifikationen, Verdienste und unseren unternehmerischen Anforderungen. Vielfältige Teams machen es möglich, die sich verändernden Bedürfnisse unserer Kunden besser zu erfüllen und für unsere Zukunft zu sorgen.

description_full:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

This role is based in Bangkok, Thailand, and is open to candidates worldwide. We offer a generous relocation package to ensure a smooth transition to living and working in Bangkok.

The Opportunity

The B2B Marketing team at Agoda is seeking a Marketing Strategy Analyst (B2B Content Strategy) to work on our content strategy efforts. The ideal candidate will play a pivotal role in defining and executing our product marketing strategy, ensuring that our products resonate with our target audience and achieve market success. You will work on various initiatives to enhance our brand visibility and create compelling narratives that resonate with our B2B partners and prospective partners which drive significant impact to the organization.

Role Overview

As the Marketing Strategy Analyst (B2B Content Strategy), you will work on the development and execution of various communication strategies aimed at B2B partners. This role includes contributing to Agoda’s comprehensive product marketing strategies, working on our core product value propositions, optimizing all content efforts and measurement tracking, crafting materials for C-suite presentations, and collaborating with stakeholders across departments to identify and showcase our strengths in the B2B travel sector.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and Implement Product Marketing Strategy: working on comprehensive strategies and materials that engage B2B audiences, elevate brand awareness, and deliver tangible business results across multiple business units.
Craft Compelling Product Messaging: Develop messaging and positioning that differentiates our products in the market.
Project Management of Cross-Functional Initiatives: Ensure alignment and effective communication among all stakeholders while managing cross-functional projects.
Performance Analysis: Analyze product and channel performance metrics to refine marketing strategies and enhance product offerings.
Enhance Sales Materials and Strategy: Design and execute go-to-market plans for product launches, including promotional campaigns, sales enablement, and training.
Conduct Market Research: Identify partner needs and market trends to develop a competitive positioning strategy.
Craft Engaging Presentations: Design and manage high-impact presentations that effectively convey Agoda’s brand story and core strategies to partners and C-suite executives.


What You’ll Need to Succeed


Extensive Experience: A minimum of 2 years in product marketing, strategy, content marketing, or related roles—preferably in the tech industry. Experience in a B2B marketing environment is a plus.
Data-Driven Approach: Strong analytical skills with the ability to analyze data (SQL knowledge is a strong plus), translate data into actionable insights, and support content strategies and value propositions.
Innovative Problem-Solving Skills: The ability to tackle complex challenges in a non-linear fashion is essential. We seek a resourceful candidate who can effectively navigate the organization to gather necessary information and implement innovative solutions while adapting to evolving scenarios and leveraging valuable resources creatively.
Strong Communication Skills: Exceptional writing and verbal communication abilities, with proficiency in creating impactful and visually appealing presentations.
Strategic Mindset: Proven experience in developing content that aligns with business objectives and enhances brand narrative.
Industry and Product Knowledge: Familiarity with the tech and travel industries, with experience in the online travel agency sector being a plus.
Technical Proficiency: Knowledge of marketing automation tools, CRM systems, and analytics software is an advantage.


Why Join Us?

At Agoda, you will play a pivotal role in shaping our communication strategies and driving growth within the B2B marketing division. If you are an innovative thinker passionate about travel technology and have the skills to elevate our brand narrative, we invite you to apply and be part of our dynamic team!

Our vision is to establish HUGO BOSS as the leading premium tech-driven fashion platform worldwide and to be one of the top 100 global brands. At HUGO BOSS, we work as a team to apply our knowledge, skills and experience together and create a diversity of ideas and solutions. What unites us? We love fashion, we change fashion!

At HUGO BOSS, you have the opportunity to contribute your personality, ideas and creativity — because only when we break new ground together can we create something unique. Become part of our team of more than 19.000 employees worldwide and shape your future at HUGO BOSS!

Are you passionate about shaping data-driven pricing strategies on a global scale? At HUGO BOSS, we are seeking a highly skilled BP&A Pricing Expert (m/f/d) to drive impactful pricing decisions across all brands, product categories, and channels. In this role, you will partner closely with top management, steer strategic pricing and budgeting processes, and evaluate new business opportunities. Join us in elevating pricing excellence and performance transparency in a dynamic, fast-paced, and international environment.

The position is limited to a fixed term of 2 years.

What you can expect:


Serve as the primary BP&A contact for all pricing-related topics within the global HUGO BOSS pricing strategy across all brands, product categories, and sales channels

At Statista, we’re all about facts and data, for we are the world's leading business data platform. By providing reliable and easy-to-use data as well as various data analytics products and services, we empower people worldwide to make fact-based decisions.

Founded in Hamburg in 2007, we have quickly grown into a global company with offices in major cities such as London, New York, Berlin and Tokyo. And we still have a lot of plans. description_full:
Our Story

Every year millions of people are either filing their taxes in fear or giving up on their tax refund altogether. We're working on fixing that. Our intuitive app enables anyone, regardless of education or background, to file their taxes with newfound confidence.

Spread across Germany, Spain and the UK, the team at Taxfix Group with its brands Taxfix, Steuerbot and TaxScouts, is a compassionate group of solution-finders. We speak our minds openly, and with over 400 professionals, including tax experts, developers, and IT security experts, we're rich in ideas and voices. The group has facilitated more than 3.5 billion euros in tax refunds for its customers since its founding in 2016.

Your Challenge

Join our Marketing Analytics team as a Data Analyst, where your mission is to transform data into strategies that fuel growth. You'll apply advanced analytics and AI to influence CLV, CAC, and revenue outcomes. As a trusted partner to Growth Marketing, CRM, and MarTech, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping how we scale efficiently in competitive markets.

Your Responsibilities And Decisions


Design, run, and improve measurement frameworks (attribution, MMM, incrementality tests, brand trackers). Use their insights to drive test-and-learn plans and guide strategic decisions on budgeting, channel mix, and retention.
Apply advanced statistical and predictive techniques, turning complex data into clear recommendations that directly influence marketing decisions to maximize CLV and reduce CAC.
Partner closely with Growth Marketing, MarTech, CRM, Finance, and Analytics Engineering to ensure data quality, model accuracy, and scalable implementation of insights.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives, dashboards, notebooks, decks, and recommendations, that drive decisions across Marketing.
Communicate insights clearly and persuasively, driving alignment across stakeholders and ensuring data-driven decisions are widely adopted.
Build and utilize AI-native analytics workflows (e.g. agentic data exploration, LLM-assisted causal analysis) and bring them into team practice.


Your Profile


2+ years in Marketing Analytics and Data Modeling, ideally in B2C acquisition and retention.
Hands-on with attribution models, MMM, incrementality testing and campaign performance analysis across digital paid channels (search, social, programmatic) and CRM. Exposure to offline/brand measurement (TV, CTV, podcast) is beneficial.
Hands-on expertise in statistical modeling and machine learning (e.g. regression, Bayesian methods, classification, clustering, forecasting), with experience in causal inference and experimentation design applied to marketing use cases such as CLV prediction, demand forecasting and pricing elasticity models.
Advanced SQL for data manipulation, Python for statistical analysis and predictive modeling, experience with dbt for data modeling and Looker (or similar BI tools) for dashboarding and reporting.
Strong ability to extract insights and connect them to CAC, CLV, and retention outcomes, guiding acquisition and retention strategies.
Skilled at distilling complex data into actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders, with experience influencing senior leaders.
Proactive, outcome-driven, and comfortable owning analyses from data pipeline to business recommendation that drives measurable business impact.
Practical experience applying AI-driven solutions such as prototyping with LLMs (e.g. Claude Code, Gemini CLI), automating workflows (e.g. LangChain, n8n), using AI-powered IDEs (e.g. Cursor) to accelerate coding and analysis, or enhancing marketing analytics processes.


Why Taxfix?


A chance to do meaningful, people-centric work with an international team of passionate professionals.
Holistic well-being with free mental health coaching sessions and yoga.
A monthly allowance to spend on an extensive range of services that you can use and roll over as flexibly as you like.
Employee stock options for all employees—because everyone deserves to benefit from the success they help to create.
30 annual vacation days and flexible working hours.
Work from abroad for up to six weeks every year. Just align with your team, and then enjoy your trip.
Plenty of opportunities to socialise as a team. In addition to internal tech meetups, our international team hosts regular get-togethers—virtually and in person when possible.
Free tax declaration filing, of course, through the Taxfix app—and internal support for all personal tax-related questions.
Have a four-legged friend in your life? We’re happy to have dogs join us in the office.


Excited? So are we. Learn more about Team Taxfix on our blog and get a glimpse of our culture.

At Taxfix, we believe that incredible things happen when you have a wealth of perspectives and experiences. We're proudly committed to equal employment and development opportunities no matter your gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, colour, disability, or place of origin. To help mitigate any potential unconscious biases, we ask that you refrain from including your picture, age, or marital status on your CV. Let your experiences speak for themselves.

Not sure if you meet all the requirements for this role? Please apply anyway. You might bring something special to the team that we hadn't considered previously.
