description_full:
As a Global Salesforce Platform Administrator - Marketing & Data Cloud, you are responsible for the design, configuration, and continuous evolution of the Marketing and Data Cloud stream within the global Customer Engagement ecosystem (CRM), with a strong focus on Marketing Cloud, and Data Cloud.

You are part of a globally distributed platform team, organized across regional hubs to ensure scalable coverage across time zones. You will work side by side with Product Owners, and global teams, supporting the delivery of scalable, harmonized solutions while ensuring the platform can be effectively adopted and extended at regional level. The role combines Business Analysis and Salesforce Administration, with a strong focus on platform enhancement, feature development, and configuration, while collaborating with local CRM specialists who drive end-user support, and training in the markets.

Your focus is on implementing and evolving data-driven and marketing automation solutions in alignment with global platform strategy, ensuring consistency, scalability, and long-term maintainability. You contribute deep functional and technical expertise across Marketing Cloud and Data Cloud, supporting both global and local stakeholders, particularly for complex use cases, advanced segmentation and activation scenarios, and the continuous evolution of the Customer Engagement ecosystem.

Responsibilities

Global Platform Development (Marketing & Data Cloud Stream)


Own and evolve the Marketing & Data Cloud streams within the global Customer Engagement ecosystem (focus on Marketing and Data Cloud), ensuring scalability, standardization, and alignment with global platform strategy
Design and implement global solutions across Marketing Cloud and Data Cloud, including marketing automation, campaign orchestration, journeys, segmentation, data models, and integrations
Collaborate closely with other platform streams (e.g., Sales, Customer Service, Technical Service) to ensure seamless end-to-end processes, consistent data flows, and a unified customer view across the ecosystem
Translate business requirements into solution designs and technical specifications, ensuring best practices and long-term maintainability
Ensure alignment with global data models, governance frameworks, consent management, and architecture standards
Continuously improve the platform by identifying opportunities for marketing automation, harmonization, and efficiency gains across marketing and data processes
Identify and drive the adoption of AI-driven capabilities (e.g., Agentforce, predictive audiences, personalization, automation) to enhance campaign effectiveness, customer engagement, and decision-making


Local Demand Definition & Implementation


Own the implementation of prioritized demands in alignment with global governance and product prioritization processes
Collaborate closely with business stakeholders, Product Owners, Business Analysts, and local CRM specialists to refine requirements and ensure implementation readiness
Validate and challenge incoming requirements to ensure scalable, reusable, and globally aligned marketing automation solutions
Support localization by adapting global campaign frameworks, data models, and activation strategies to market-specific needs while maintaining overall standardization
Contribute to workshops and alignment sessions to ensure smooth translation of business needs into global solutions and effective deployment into operations
Evaluate and prioritize demands with a focus on leveraging AI capabilities where applicable, ensuring scalable, value-driven, and future-oriented solutions


Commercial Excellence, Collaboration Model & Regional Hub Setup


Operate as part of a globally distributed Salesforce platform team, organized across regional hubs to ensure coverage across time zones
Collaborate seamlessly across regions, with shared responsibility for all countries and markets, both in daily operations and platform evolution
Act as the functional expert for Marketing Cloud and Data Cloud, supporting local CRM specialists with deep platform, marketing automation and data expertise when required
Work closely with IT, Commercial Excellence, and Digital Transformation teams to ensure alignment across the broader ecosystem
Lead and support cross-regional CRM initiatives and process optimizations within the wider Customer Engagement ecosystem, incl. marketing effectiveness, customer engagement


Data Analytics & Operational Business Support


Ensure data quality, consistency, and integrity across Marketing Cloud and Data Cloud, including identity resolution, data harmonization, and governance
Enable advanced reporting, analytics, and AI-driven insights by ensuring proper data structure, segmentation capabilities, and activation readiness
Support the development of training materials and enablement concepts, and contribute to training sessions where required (e.g., complex journeys, segmentation logic, AI use cases), in close collaboration with local teams
Provide expert-level support for complex or escalated topics, particularly in areas such as Journey Builder, campaign automation, segmentation logic, data integration, and AI-driven use cases, enabling local teams to design and operate effective customer journeys


Requisitions


At least 5 years of professional experience in the Pharma / Life Sciences industry, ideally in CRM, commercial excellence, or customer engagement environments
At least 3 years of hands-on experience working with Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Data Cloud, or similar marketing automation / CDP platforms
University degree in business administration, economics, IT, or a related field
Strong experience with Marketing Cloud (e.g., journeys, automation, segmentation) and ideally Data Cloud (e.g., data modeling, identity resolution, activation)
Proven experience in translating business requirements into scalable marketing and data solutions within a global platform environment
Experience working within global governance models and collaborating with distributed, cross-regional teams
Salesforce Marketing Cloud Admin/Consultant Certification and/or Business Analyst required; additional certifications such as Data Cloud, Agentforce are a plus
Solid understanding of data models, integrations, consent management, and reporting capabilities
Experience with agile delivery models and tools such as JIRA and Confluence
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, particularly in data-driven and customer-centric use cases
Experience or strong interest in leveraging AI-driven capabilities
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and business teams
Structured, independent, and results-oriented working style
Fluency in English required, any additional languages are an advantage
Intercultural experience, ability to work in a global environment, and willingness to travel internationally

description_full:
About You

You are a data-driven, creative marketer who thrives on building campaigns that generate real pipeline. You're equally comfortable designing a scalable 1:many nurture program as you are crafting a personalized 1:few play for a strategic account segment. You bring a builder's mindset — you don't just execute campaigns, you architect the strategies and goals behind them — and you measure your success in sales-qualified opportunities and revenue.

About Us

Constructor is the next-generation platform for search and discovery in ecommerce, built to explicitly optimize for metrics like revenue, conversion rate, and profit. Our search engine is entirely invented in-house utilizing transformers and generative LLMs, and we use its core and personalization capabilities to power everything from search itself to recommendations to shopping agents. Engineering is by far our largest department, and we've built our proprietary engine to be the best on the market, having never lost an A/B test to a competitive technology. We're passionate about maintaining this and work on the bleeding edge of AI to do so.

Out of necessity, our engine is built for extreme scale and powers over 1 billion queries every day across 150 languages and roughly 100 countries. It is used by some of the biggest ecommerce companies in the world like Sephora, Under Armour, and Petco.

We're a passionate team who love solving problems and want to make our customers' and coworkers' lives better. We value empathy, openness, curiosity, continuous improvement, and are excited by metrics that matter. We believe that empowering everyone in a company to do what they do best can lead to great things.

Constructor is a U.S. based company that has been in the market since 2019. It was founded by Eli Finkelshteyn and Dan McCormick who still lead the company today.

About The Position

As our Demand Generation Manager, you will own the strategy and execution of multi-channel campaigns that drive sales-qualified opportunities (SQOs) across the funnel. You'll build the 1:few program — including email nurtures, webinars, and paid media — to build brand awareness and generate pipeline in high value accounts. You'll also work closely with the Demand Generation Lead on select 1:many campaigns.

You'll operate at the intersection of marketing strategy, technology, and analytics — leveraging our demand gen tech stack (HubSpot, 6sense, Salesforce), building audience segments, and continuously testing and iterating to improve conversion at every stage. You'll own campaign performance reporting and be accountable for pipeline contribution and SQO targets. This role is on the Growth Marketing team and works cross-functionally with Sales, Content, and Product Marketing.

Requirements


2-4 years of experience in B2B demand generation or growth marketing, with a demonstrated track record of driving pipeline and sales-qualified opportunities
Hands-on proficiency with HubSpot, 6sense, and Salesforce
Experience designing and executing both 1:many scaled programs (email nurtures, webinars, paid campaigns) and 1:few account-based plays for targeted segments
Strong analytical skills with the ability to build campaign reporting, interpret funnel metrics, and make data-driven optimization decisions
Excellent cross-functional collaboration skills, particularly with Sales — you know how to align on target account lists, SQO definitions, and feedback loops
Experience with A/B testing, audience segmentation, and multi-touch attribution in a SaaS or technology company
Availability to collaborate across U.S. and European time zones as needed to support a globally distributed team


Benefits


🏝️ Unlimited vacation time - we strongly encourage all of our employees take at least 3 weeks per year
💰 A competitive compensation package including stock options
🌎 Fully remote team - choose where you live
🛋️ Work from home stipend! We want you to have the resources you need to set up your home office
💻 Apple laptops provided for new employees
🧑‍🎓 Training and development budget for every employee, refreshed each year
👪 Maternity & Paternity leave for qualified employees
🧠 Work with smart people who will help you grow and make a meaningful impact
🏥 Company sponsored US health coverage (100% paid for employee)

description_full:
Du willst nicht nur einen einzelnen Kanal optimieren, sondern den Marktauftritt einer ganzen Marke zielgerichtet ausbauen? Du verstehst Marketing als das, was es heute ist — die Orchestrierung aller Kanäle für systematische Demand Generation, kombiniert mit dem cleveren Einsatz von AI-Tools, um Sichtbarkeit und Lead-Generierung auf das nächste Level zu heben? Dann ist das deine Rolle.




Bei Siloy DACH bündeln wir mit TRIALTA und LEVR die führende HubSpot-Expertise im deutschsprachigen Raum - als Teil der europäischen Siloy-Plattform und HubSpot Global Partner of the Year.

Für unser Wachstum suchen wir einen Growth Marketing Manager (m/w/d), der/die unseren eigenen Auftritt strategisch und operativ ausbaut und die Demand Generation für Mid-Market (50–300 MA) und Enterprise (bis ca. 3.000 MA) verantwortet.

Optimalerweise an unserem Standort in Berlin oder Hannover.




Deine Aufgaben




Demand Generation orchestrieren: Du baust unseren Marktauftritt zielgerichtet aus und führst Paid (LinkedIn, Google, Meta), SEO/Content, Webinare und Events zu einer abgestimmten Lead-Engine zusammen — statt isolierter Kanal-Maßnahmen.
AI-Tools für Sichtbarkeit produktiv einsetzen: Du setzt moderne AI-Tools strategisch ein, um unsere Reichweite zu skalieren — von Content-Produktion über GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) bis zu Kampagnen-Automatisierung. Wir wollen hier vorne dabei sein, nicht nur mitlaufen.
Pipeline systematisch aufbauen: Du verantwortest die Pipeline-Kontribution aus Marketing — von der Kanalauswahl über die Kampagnenplanung bis zur Übergabe an Sales. CPL, MQL-Cost und Pipeline-Beitrag sind deine Leitwährungen.
Zielgruppen-Splitting Mid-Market vs. Enterprise: Du differenzierst klar zwischen Mid-Market (Self-Service-tauglich, schnellere Zyklen) und Enterprise (ABM-Logik, längere Buying Committees) und fährst die jeweils passenden Kanäle und Inhalte.
Reporting & Attribution in HubSpot: Du machst die Wirkung deiner Arbeit transparent — auch über die langen Multi-Touch-Customer-Journeys hinweg, die im B2B Realität sind.
Optional: Unterstützung in Kundenprojekten: Wo es passt und du Lust hast, kannst du dein Know-how situativ auch in Kundenprojekten einbringen — als interner Sparringspartner oder direkt im Delivery.




Dein Profil




3–6 Jahre Erfahrung im B2B-Growth- oder Demand-Generation-Marketing — bei einer Agentur, einem SaaS-Unternehmen oder im Inhouse-Marketing-Team.
Orchestrierungs-Mindset: Du denkst in Funnels und Kanal-Mixes, nicht in Einzelkampagnen. Du erkennst, an welchem Hebel als nächstes zu drehen ist, und kannst das auch begründen.
Hands-on Paid-Media-Erfahrung: Du hast LinkedIn-, Google- und Meta-Kampagnen mit relevanten Budgets selbst aufgesetzt und optimiert — auch wenn deine Rolle bei uns mehr Steuerung als Klick-Arbeit sein wird.
AI-Affinität mit Substanz: Du arbeitest aktiv mit modernen AI-Tools, hast eine Meinung dazu, was funktioniert und was Marketing-Theater ist, und experimentierst eigenständig mit neuen Ansätzen.
SEO/GEO-Verständnis: Du verstehst Keyword-Strategie, technisches SEO in Grundzügen und hast eine Haltung dazu, wie sich Sichtbarkeit unter LLM-Suchverhalten verändert.
Webinar- und Event-Routine: Mindestens ein Format hast du selbst von Konzept bis Follow-up gebaut — idealerweise mit messbarem Pipeline-Beitrag.
HubSpot-Affinität: Du musst nicht alle Workflows blind bauen können, aber du arbeitest täglich darin — Marketing Hub, UTM-Tracking, Reporting, Listen, Segmentierung. Wer HubSpot in der Tiefe kennt, hat bei uns einen klaren Vorteil.
Datengetriebenes Denken: Du triffst Entscheidungen auf Basis von Zahlen, nicht Bauchgefühl — und kannst sie auch erklären.
Sprachen: Verhandlungssicheres Deutsch zwingend, sehr gutes Englisch für die Zusammenarbeit innerhalb der europäischen Siloy-Gruppe.




Warum wir?




Eine echte B2B-Marke mit Substanz: Siloy ist HubSpot Global Partner of the Year. TRIALTA und LEVR haben in DACH einen ausgezeichneten Ruf — du baust auf einem Fundament auf, nicht auf der grünen Wiese.
Gestaltungsspielraum statt Schubladenrolle: Du verantwortest unseren Marketing-Auftritt mit echtem Mandat. Strategie, Budget, Kanalmix, Tooling — du gestaltest, statt vorgegebene Playbooks abzuarbeiten.
Budget, das wirkt: Wir investieren ernsthaft in Demand Generation. Du bekommst die Mittel, um Kanäle ordentlich zu testen und AI-Tools produktiv einzuführen.
Tech-Stack ohne Kompromisse: HubSpot Marketing Hub Enterprise, sauber aufgesetzt von Leuten, die HubSpot beruflich implementieren. Dazu die AI- und Marketing-Tools, die du wirklich brauchst.
Europäisches Peer-Netzwerk: Du tauschst dich mit Marketing-Kollegen aus den anderen Siloy-Regionen aus — Benelux, Nordics, Schweiz, UK&I. Best Practices statt Bubble.
Hybrides Arbeiten: Office-Tage in Berlin, Hannover oder Nürnberg, ansonsten flexibel. Regional treffen wir uns regelmäßig, einmal jährlich kommt die ganze Siloy-Gruppe zusammen.
Attraktives Gehaltspaket: Fixum plus zielbasierter Komponente, betriebliche Altersvorsorge und die üblichen Benefits — Details besprechen wir im Erstgespräch.
Weiterbildung mit Substanz: INBOUND-Konferenz, HubSpot-Zertifizierungen, Konferenzbudget. Wir glauben, dass gute Marketer ständig lernen.

description_full:
Omio’s vision is to enable people to travel seamlessly anywhere, anyway. We are bringing all  global transport into a single distribution system and creating end-to-end magical consumer journeys.



With Omio you can compare and book trains, buses, ferries and flights anywhere in Europe, the US, and Canada with one simple search, on mobile, app or desktop. By offering transparent pricing and easy booking, Omio makes travel planning simple, flexible and personal.



Omio is the largest source of inventory, with 1000+ suppliers operating in 46 countries. Our brands also include Rome2Rio for global discovery, while Omio powers ticketing and journeys, which attract 900m+ users every year. Our offices are based in Berlin, Prague, Melbourne, Brazil, Bangalore, and London.



We are a growing team of more than 400 passionate employees from more than 50 countries who share the same vision: to create a single tool to help send travellers almost anywhere in the world.





Job Description



We are currently looking for a ​​​​​​Growth Marketing Manager in our Berlin office.



As a Growth Marketing Manager at Omio, you own the growth and commercial success of a defined market or region. You are responsible for identifying, prioritizing and scaling the most effective growth levers across the full user journey, with a strong focus on paid acquisition across web and app, partnerships, and search-driven growth.







Working closely with cross-functional stakeholders, you define the growth strategy for your market, make trade-offs between scale and efficiency, and drive disciplined, sustainable user and revenue growth through experimentation, data-driven decision making, and continuous learning.



What you will work on



End-to-End ownership: Own growth performance and commercial success for a defined market or region, driving user and revenue growth by defining, prioritizing, and scaling high-impact levers across the full user journey.

You will collaborate and align with the Expansion team/STOs.



Growth strategy: Develop and execute market-specific growth strategies across:



Paid Search (SEM)



Paid App channels (e.g. Apple Search Ads, Google App campaigns)



Affiliate and Influencer Marketing



SEO and ASO (strategic oversight and optimization, not full content ownership)





Campaign & Investment Management: Own end-to-end campaign and investment execution for your market, including budgeting, forecasting, and optimizing scale vs. efficiency, while identifying and scaling new growth opportunities through structured experimentation and rapid iteration.



Local Market Adaptation: Own creative and messaging performance for your market by translating global playbooks into locally relevant growth initiatives. Partner with local agencies and internal teams to test hypotheses, refine localisation, and improve performance based on market-specific insights.



Affiliate & Influencer Management: Oversee affiliate and influencer performance for your market, along with the Affiliate team, including partner onboarding, performance evaluation, and ROI optimization.



Stakeholder Management & Influence: Build strong working relationships across Growth, Expansion, Product, Analytics, and senior commercial stakeholders. Navigate differing perspectives, align on priorities, and communicate recommendations clearly in a fast-moving, high-expectation environment.



Data & Measurement: Leverage tracking and analytics infrastructure to ensure accurate attribution, measurement, and insight generation, and use data to inform decision making, trade-offs, and next steps.





Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.


Strong experience in performance and growth marketing, with a proven track record and hands-on expertise in scaling paid acquisition across major platforms (especially Google Ads), including web and app environments.


Experience managing or scaling Affiliate and/or Influencer Marketing programs, including partner evaluation, optimization, and ROI-driven decision-making.


Working knowledge of SEO and ASO fundamentals, with the ability to apply these insights within a broader growth strategy.


Strong analytical skills with a deep understanding of growth KPIs, attribution models, and performance metrics; experienced in working with large datasets and using tools like Excel (or similar) to generate insights and drive decisions.

Knowledge of SQL, Python, or similar tools is a plus.


Experience with campaign automation, testing, and experimentation frameworks, and the ability to apply learnings at scale.


Proven ability to work effectively with senior stakeholders, manage competing priorities, and communicate clearly in high-pressure or ambiguous environments.


Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work cross-functionally, align stakeholders, and drive decisions.


Additional Information



What’s in it for you?



Have an impact. With innovation and smart technology, we are changing the way people travel, and you too can have an impact on this ride
A competitive and attractive compensation package
A diverse team of more than 50 nationalities
and more

Diversity makes us stronger

description_full:
Germany – Marketing Manager

Department: Marketing

Reporting to: Head of Marketing Europe & Country Manager

Location: Germany (flexible, with regular travel)






The Role






TVS Motor is on the lookout for a bold Marketing Manager to fuel its European expansion—elevating brand visibility in Germany, skyrocketing web traffic, and turning digital interactions into real growth.




If you love fast-paced environments and shaping what’s next, this role is for you.






The Marketing Manager will be responsible for developing and implementing marketing strategies in Germany to enhance brand awareness, drive customer engagement, and support sales growth.

The ideal candidate will report directly to the Country Manager and the Head of Marketing Europe and work closely with dealers to ensure alignment with business objectives.






Duties/Responsibilities




Partner with the Head of Marketing EU & Country Manager to develop and deliver local and regional marketing plans that drive brand growth and customer acquisition, in line with Head Office strategy.
Collaborate closely with sales teams to design and execute effective, localised marketing campaigns aligned with EU and Head Office guidelines.
A European expansion vision: structuring and strengthening a scalable and distinctive brand platform across multiple markets.
Plan and manage local and regional on & offline advertising, promotional activity.
Define and implement the digital marketing strategy, proposing innovative solutions and tools to enhance the effectiveness of marketing initiatives.
Manage and optimize the conversion funnel and lead the operational deployment of digital marketing actions aimed at generating leads and increasing visibility.
Develop acquisition, loyalty, and retention strategies together with our in-house expert
Publish editorial content (press articles, videos, infographics, visuals, blog posts, and product pages for the website), run email marketing campaigns, and manage and animate social media channels (LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram).
Contribute to content strategy for digital campaigns
Build and maintain strong relationships with dealer network, supporting and enabling their local marketing efforts.
Monitor, track, and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) to assess campaign performance and effectiveness.
Ensure consistent brand representation and compliance with corporate marketing standards.
Manage marketing budgets effectively to maximise return on investment (ROI).
Work with external agencies, media partners, and suppliers to deliver high-quality marketing execution.






Personal Specification




6 years of experience in a similar role, with significant 360 marketing experience and a good understanding of web and digital ecosystems.
Demonstrated experience managing regional or multi-location marketing activity across diverse markets.
Strong hands-on experience across dealer marketing, digital marketing, social media, and customer engagement strategies.
Proven track record of delivering integrated marketing campaigns that drive brand awareness, lead generation, and customer acquisition.






Competencies




Demonstrate autonomy and intellectual curiosity. Strong organizational skills and driven of change. A strong hands-on mindset.
Dynamic, proactive, and share values of teamwork.
Strong interest in new digital technologies.
Fluent in English well organized and have solid writing skills.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Works effectively with sales teams, corporate stakeholders, and external partners to deliver aligned outcomes.
Project & Priority Management: Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
Stakeholder Partnership: Builds trusted relationships with distributors, dealers, agencies, and suppliers to enable effective execution.
Adaptability & Ownership: comfortable operating with autonomy, navigating ambiguity, and taking ownership of outcomes.






At TVS, every application receives careful consideration. If you have not heard from us within four weeks, we may be progressing with other candidates for this role. That said, we would be delighted to stay connected and welcome your interest in future opportunities with us.

description_full:
An den Speichern 8, 48157 Münster

ab 01.07.2026

Gestalte mit uns eine Welt, in der Inhalt zählt.

Bei Thalia glauben wir daran, dass Bücher und Geschichten Menschen bereichern und verbinden. Seit über 100 Jahren schaffen wir Raum für Ideen und Inhalte, die bewegen und inspirieren – in unseren über 500 Buchhandlungen, online und digital. Wir möchten, dass Lesen einfach zugänglich und erlebbar ist, und begleiten unsere Kundinnen und Kunden auf ihrem persönlichen Weg zu neuen Welten und Perspektiven.

Unser Team von rund 6.800 Mitarbeitenden lebt diesen Anspruch jeden Tag. Wir übernehmen Verantwortung, teilen Wissen, lernen voneinander und wachsen gemeinsam. Wenn du Lust hast, Teil eines Teams zu sein, das das Lesen liebt und mit Leidenschaft daran arbeitet, den Zugang zu wertvollen Inhalten für alle Menschen zu ermöglichen, dann freuen wir uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen. Werde Teil unserer Thalia-Erfolgstory und bringe deine Ideen und deine Begeisterung aktiv ein – für eine Welt, in der Inhalt zählt.

Deine Mission bei uns

Als Junior Manager CRM (m/w/d) gestaltest du datengetriebene Kundenkommunikation entlang des gesamten Customer Lifecycles. Du entwickelst zielgerichtete Kampagnen, analysierst deren Performance und optimierst Customer Journeys kanalübergreifend. Gemeinsam mit Analytics, Content, Shopmanagement und Tech-Teams sorgst du für relevante, kreative und wirkungsvolle Kundenerlebnisse.

Das bewegst du konkret


CRM-Kampagnen konzipieren & umsetzen: Du planst, steuerst und optimierst CRM-Kampagnen in Braze über verschiedene Kanäle wie E-Mail, Push oder In-App zur Steigerung von Kundenbindung und Aktivierung.
Customer Journeys weiterentwickeln: Du unterstützt bei der Pflege und Optimierung automatisierter Customer Journeys entlang des gesamten Customer Lifecycles und bringst neue Ideen zur Weiterentwicklung ein.
Zielgruppen strategisch entwickeln: Du erstellst eigenständig Selektionen und Segmentierungen und entwickelst datenbasierte Zielgruppenstrategien für relevante und personalisierte Kommunikation.
Performance analysieren & optimieren: Du monitorst Kampagnenergebnisse anhand relevanter KPIs, leitest konkrete Handlungsempfehlungen ab und setzt Optimierungsmaßnahmen kontinuierlich um.
Testing & Qualität sichern: Du führst A/B-Tests durch, dokumentierst CRM-Maßnahmen sorgfältig und stellst eine hohe Qualität in der operativen Umsetzung sicher.
Cross-funktional zusammenarbeiten: Du arbeitest eng mit Analytics, Content, Shopmanagement und Tech-Teams zusammen und unterstützt die Umsetzung datengetriebener Marketingmaßnahmen.


Das bringst du mit


Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Marketing oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
Erste praktische Erfahrungen im CRM- oder Kampagnenmanagement, z. B. durch Praktika oder Werkstudententätigkeiten
Grundverständnis für datengetriebenes Marketing sowie Interesse an Customer Engagement und Lifecycle-Kommunikation
Idealerweise erste Berührungspunkte mit CRM-Tools wie Braze, Salesforce, Emarsys oder vergleichbaren Plattformen
Analytisches Denkvermögen sowie Interesse an Zielgruppensegmentierung, Kampagnenlogik und datenbasierter Optimierung
Strukturierte, eigenständige und sorgfältige Arbeitsweise mit hoher Lernbereitschaft und Hands-on-Mentalität
Kommunikationsstärke, Teamfähigkeit und Begeisterung für kreative sowie relevante Kundenkommunikation in cross-funktionalen Teams


Das bieten wir dir

Neben vielen klassischen Vorteilen wie Company Bike, ÖPNV-Vergünstigung oder Personalrabatt findest du bei uns:


Gestaltungsspielraum: Verwirkliche deine Ideen und gestalte aktiv die Zukunft von Thalia mit.
Persönliches Wachstum: Profitiere von diversen Weiterbildungsangeboten und individuellen Karrierewegen.
Sinnstiftende Arbeit: Dein Einsatz hat direkten Einfluss auf unsere digitale Kompetenz – insbesondere in den Bereichen eCom, IT-Security & -Maintenance.
Agiles Arbeiten und moderne Unternehmenskultur: Arbeite flexibel und eigenverantwortlich in einem offenen, dynamischen Umfeld.
Teamspirit: Erlebe Zusammenarbeit und gegenseitige Unterstützung auf allen Ebenen.
Vielfalt und Offenheit: Wir schätzen unterschiedliche Perspektiven und fördern Diversität.
Treiber des Wandels: Gestalte als Teil von #TeamThalia mit uns die Transformation unseres Unternehmens.


In der Welt der Bücher ist Platz für jede Geschichte und jede Perspektive. Bei Thalia sind wir fest davon überzeugt, dass Vielfalt und eine Kultur der Wertschätzung entscheidende Antriebskräfte für Kreativität und Innovation sind. Daher begrüßen wir ausdrücklich Bewerbungen von Menschen jeglicher Herkunft, Religion, Geschlechtsidentität, sexuellen Orientierung, Alter und Fähigkeiten. Auch Teilzeit-Anstellungen sind bei uns herzlich willkommen.

description_full:
Hey there!

We’re Fever, the world’s leading tech platform for culture and live entertainment.

Our mission? To democratize access to culture and entertainment. With our proprietary cutting-edge technology and data-driven approach, we’re revolutionizing the way people engage with live entertainment.

Every month, our platform inspires over 300 million people in +55 countries (and counting) to discover unforgettable experiences while also empowering event creators with our data and technology, helping them scale, innovate, and enhance their events to reach new audiences.

Our results? We’ve teamed up with major industry leaders like Netflix, F.C. Barcelona, and Primavera Sound, presented international award-winning experiences, and are backed by several leading global investors! Impressive, right?

To achieve our mission, we are looking for bar-raisers with a hands-on mindset who are eager to help shape the future of entertainment!

Ready to be part of the experience?

Now, let’s discuss this role and what you will do to help achieve Fever’s mission.

Meet the Team

The Marketing & Growth team is one of the largest and most dynamic at Fever, playing a central role in driving visibility, engagement, and business impact across the company. We work closely with nearly every department, combining strategy, creativity, and data to connect millions of people with unforgettable experiences.

Join us if you want to drive global impact, master data-driven creativity, and help millions of people discover their next unforgettable experience.

The Role

As a Growth Senior Analyst, you will independently lead high-impact global projects and strategies to drive Fever's entertainment market expansion across up to 40 countries. By designing end-to-end solutions and managing new business streams, you will collaborate across departments to enhance overall company performance.

What You'll Do


In this role, you will contribute to one of Fever's key markets, focusing on its growth and development.
Being a Growth Senior Analyst at Fever means working independently and managing your own pipeline of projects within a business line where you can showcase the value you bring.
Take part in the exponential growth of fever by taking the lead on global projects that will define the future of the entertainment industry
Enhance the overall company performance across different business departments which work together in the creation of new plans and events
Define and establish new strategies to increase performance using analytical, business and strategic skills
Design end-to-end solutions with daily high impact
Create, develop and manage new projects like new business streams, workflows, strategies, and specific departments' growth
Work with people and projects across different markets, with a special focus on developing new experiences across markets, in a big international atmosphere (up to 40 countries)


Who You Are


You have a bachelor's in Engineering (civil, industrial, telecommunications, or aeronautics) or a background in Maths, Statistics, Data analytics, or Strategy.
At least 3 - 5 years experience in a business analytics role.
A high level of English is required. Spanish is a plus.
Experience in strategy consulting, investment banking, and strategy or analytics roles at top tech firms is a significant advantage
Data-driven person who brings numbers to strategic and business decisions
Generate high business impact based on your decisions
Excellent interpersonal skills with a proven ability to manage and influence multiple stakeholders while working effectively across teams, disciplines, and business cases.

description_full:
Wir suchen für unser Team in München oder Berlin eine Junior Research & Insights Manager (w/m/d) befristet auf 18 Monate.

Wir sind eine globale, socially-led Creative Agency mit über 1.300 Mitarbeitenden in 17 Büros weltweit. In einer Welt, in der Social Media Kultur prägt, helfen wir Marken dabei, Ideen zu entwickeln, über die Menschen sprechen.

Wir verstehen Online-Communities, Kulturen und Subkulturen über die gesamte Social-Landschaft hinweg. Zu unseren Kund:innen in Deutschland zählen unter anderem AUDI AG, Mercedes-Benz Trucks und Müller. We Are Social ist Teil der Plus Company.

Als Junior Research & Insights Manager (w/m/d) unterstützt du unser Intelligence Team bei unterschiedlichen Kund:innenprojekten und arbeitest eng mit erfahrenen Kolleg:innen aus Research, Strategie und Data zusammen. Dabei lernst du Schritt für Schritt, wie datenbasierte Insights entstehen und wie sie kreative und strategische Entscheidungen unterstützen.

Requirements

Deine Aufgaben:


Unterstützung bei der Recherche und Analyse von Social-Media-, Markt- und Zielgruppendaten
Mitarbeit bei der Erstellung von Reports, Präsentationen und Benchmark-Analysen
Unterstützung bei der Pflege und Aktualisierung von Dashboards
Aufbereitung und Visualisierung von Daten und Erkenntnissen für interne und externe Präsentationen
Beobachtung von digitalen Trends, Plattformen und kulturellen Entwicklungen
Unterstützung bei der Analyse von Creator- und Influencer-Aktivitäten
Zusammenarbeit mit dem Strategy-, Research- und Data-Team bei unterschiedlichen Kund:innenprojekten
Schrittweises Lernen und Verstehen von KPI-Analysen, Insight-Entwicklung und datenbasierter Strategiearbeit


Wonach suchen wir?


Erste praktische Erfahrung im Agentur-, Marketing-, Research- oder Social-Media-Umfeld
Großes Interesse an Social Media, digitalen Trends und Online-Kultur
Interesse an Daten, Kommunikation, Insights und Konsumentenverhalten
Motivation, neue Tools und Plattformen schnell zu lernen
Strukturierte, zuverlässige und eigenständige Arbeitsweise
Kommunikationsstärke und Spaß an Teamarbeit
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift


Benefits

Warum du Teil unseres Teams werden solltest?

Lebendiger Teamgeist: Wir tragen das Adjektiv social sehr bewusst im Namen und leben einen starken Teamgeist und eine offene Unternehmenskultur mit flachen Hierarchien, in denen deine Stimme zählt!

Spannende Projekte mit internationaler Reichweite: Wir arbeiten an digitalen Projekten, die Maßstäbe setzen! Unsere Kund:innen kommen aus dem B2C- und B2B-Umfeld und sind sowohl lokale als auch internationale Player.

Global verbunden und lokal verwurzelt: Unsere Teams an den Standorten München und Kreuzberg profitieren von einem nachhaltigen Wissensaustausch mit den WAS Standorten weltweit und sind im Glockenbachviertel und Kreuzberg zu Hause.

Flexibilität - Yes please!: Hybrides Arbeiten vereint für uns die Vorteile des Home Offices mit denen des Arbeitens im Büro. Genau deswegen haben wir uns für einen 2/3 Split entschieden, der bedeutet, dass wir bei 40h mindestens 2 Tage vor Ort mit unseren Kolleg:innen und 3 Tage im Home Office arbeiten. But that's not all! Darüber hinaus sind wir große Fans von Remote Arbeit. Deswegen hast du bei uns die Möglichkeit 20 Tage innerhalb Deutschlands und weitere 20 Tage innerhalb Europas remote zu arbeiten. Wenn du also kein großer Fan des Deutschen Winters bist, join us und du kannst diese Zeit in Portugal oder Griechenland genießen.

Mehr Benefits für dich: Weil wir wissen, wie wichtig Well-being ist und wir Benefits einfach lieben, bieten wir dir mehr als nur den Standard. Genieße 2 zusätzliche Health Days, Menstrual Leave, einen freien Tag an deinem Geburtstag, eine Kooperation mit Wellpass, das Deutschlandticket, eine Mitgliedschaft bei OpenUp und vieles mehr.

Data Insights Manager (w/m/d)

DEU-Heidenheim

In dieser Position übernehmen Sie eine Schlüsselrolle bei der Weiterentwicklung unserer Analytics- und Data-Insights-Landschaft. Sie schaffen Transparenz, liefern entscheidungsrelevante Erkenntnisse und unterstützen Vertrieb, Marketing sowie das Management dabei, fundierte und datengetriebene Entscheidungen zu treffen. Wenn Sie diese Aufgabe reizt und Sie Ihre analytische Expertise wirkungsvoll einbringen möchten, freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung.

Aufgaben


Als zentraler Treiber für Analytics & Data Insights verantworten Sie die Konzeption, Weiterentwicklung sowie den Betrieb von Analytics-, Reporting- und Dashboard-Lösungen und stellen eine transparente, konsistente Steuerung für Vertrieb und Marketing sicher

Als Analytics Sparringspartner beraten Sie Fachbereiche und Management bei der Interpretation von Reports und Insights und unterstützen fundierte, datengetriebene Entscheidungen
Im Rahmen funktionsübergreifender und internationaler Projekte wirken Sie an der Weiterentwicklung der Analytics-Architektur, KPI-Definitionen und unternehmensweiten Reporting-Standards mit

Fundierte Kenntnisse in Power BI oder SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC); erste Erfahrungen mit Databricks sind von Vorteil
Ausgeprägte Projektmanagement-Fähigkeiten sowie eine strukturierte, eigenständige Arbeitsweise
Sehr gutes Stakeholder-Management und Erfahrung im Umgang mit unterschiedlichen Zielgruppen
Fähigkeit, komplexe Sachverhalte überzeugend zu präsentieren und andere für datengetriebene Lösungen zu begeistern
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

description_full:
Wir lieben Musik und arbeiten unermüdlich daran, die Menschen zu unterstützen, die sie machen.

Hört sich gut an? Werde Teil unseres Teams!

Job Title: Junior Manager, Marketing Enablement (EMEA)

Reports To: Vice President, Marketing and Platform Enablement

Department: Global Marketing

Location:  Berlin, DE or London, UK

Your Role

The Junior Marketing Enablement Manager (EMEA) will execute and support the implementation of WMG’s marketing enablement strategy for key projects across the region, ensuring that local and regional marketing teams have the tools, platforms, and partner services required to deliver campaigns effectively, efficiently, and at scale.

Reporting to the VP of Marketing Enablement, this role will execute WMG’s marketing technology stack and vendor partnership approach in EMEA,A critical component of this role involves partnering closely with the EMEA Strategy & Operations Manager to align on regional priorities, ensuring the seamless rollout of tools and training to drive excellent product adoption.This position requires a hands-on, junior marketing manager, with operational expertise, knowledge of marketing technology and data-driven enablement in a multinational entertainment environment. As a regional team member, they will focus primarily on execution, providing data and insight to inform strategic partners, assist adoption of new tools and ways of working, and ensure enablement directly contributes to breaking artists, deepening fan engagement, and delivering commercial growth.

Here You’ll Get To

Strategy


Assist the VP of Marketing Enablement in gathering information to contribute to the global martech strategy, vendor partnerships, and supporting workflows.
Partner and align with the EMEA Strategy & Operations Manager to synchronize the regional roadmap, ensuring enablement initiatives support broader business operations.
Act as a point of contact for regional teams, escalating issues and supporting communication between regional marketing teams and the central team.
Support the martech and services innovation roadmap across EMEA, piloting new technologies and vendor models that strengthen WMG’s marketing capabilities and set the region apart.
Support change management and adoption efforts to embed new platforms, vendor services, and enablement practices across diverse markets.


Tooling & Platform Enablement


Coordinate the rollout and deployment of new tools and training programs in collaboration with regional operations leadership to ensure high engagement and product proficiency.
Support the project management, deployment, and operational maintenance of specific martech platforms within the EMEA region, ensuring platforms integrate seamlessly into the wider enterprise ecosystem.
Balance global consistency with local flexibility, implementing regional or market-specific solutions where required.
Drive adoption and effective usage of platforms through enablement, training, and best-practice sharing across regional teams.
Ensure adoption of martech solutions translates into stronger campaign execution, fan engagement, and measurable artist growth.


Third-Party Service Providers


Assist in coordinating activities with third-party marketing vendor partners for specific projects across EMEA, ensuring cost-effectiveness, high-quality service delivery, and alignment with global standards.
Collaborate with central leadership to understand contracts, manage budgets, monitor vendor performance, and optimize service offerings.
Identify and fill regional service gaps, scaling both global and local providers to meet diverse market needs while ensuring integration into WMG’s martech stack.
Build strong, trusted relationships with vendor partners, ensuring WMG has access to best-in-class services at competitive value.


Data & Measurement


Partner with Tech and Marketing Intelligence to ensure first- and third-party data is centralized, accurate, compliant (GDPR and other regional laws), and actionable.
Prepare reports and dashboards using existing analytics tools to track platform adoption and campaign performance metrics across EMEA.
Provide insights to optimize marketing enablement investments, ensuring spend delivers measurable ROI.


Innovation & Best Practices


Support the Martech roadmap across EMEA, piloting new tools, platforms, and vendor services that can strengthen WMG’s marketing capabilities.
Research and document emerging martech, ad tech, and AI-driven solutions to support the innovation roadmap, while ensuring alignment with global standards.
Share best practices across the region to scale successful initiatives and maintain consistency with WMG’s global approach.
Partner with the VP of Marketing Enablement to set and deliver the roadmap for realizing opportunities from agentic AI and other automation solutions.


About You


1-3 years of experience in a support, analyst, or coordinator role within marketing operations or technology.
Exposure to or demonstrated ability to assist with third-party vendor management and platform integration tasks.
Demonstrated success deploying and optimizing a composable marketing technology stack across multiple markets.
Analytical skills with experience preparing reports and dashboards using data.
Experience working collaboratively within cross-functional teams and supporting complex projects.
Strong communication and ability to support stakeholder communication.
Familiarity with marketing platforms, CRM systems, data management tools, and analytics solutions.
Strategic thinker with the ability to balance operational efficiency, technology adoption, and fan engagement objectives.
Passionate about music and fan engagement, with the ability to connect enablement outcomes to artist and commercial success.


We’d Love It If You Also Had


Experience in creative, music, or entertainment industries preferred.


Was uns wichtig ist und was nicht

Foto, Alter, Familienstand, Herkunft - all diese Infos brauchen wir nicht. Deine Talente und Persönlichkeit zählen bei uns! Lass uns wissen, was Dich einzigartig macht, was Du beitragen kannst und warum Warner Music mit Dir den nächsten Hit landet – wie Du uns das zeigst, ist Dir überlassen.

Datenschutzhinweis

Bei der Erstellung eines Benutzeraccounts und der Bewerbung auf eine offene Position verarbeitet Warner Music Deine persönlichen Daten gemäß der hier verfügbaren Datenschutzerklärung. Mit dem Absenden Deiner Bewerbung bestätigst Du, dass Du den Datenschutzhinweis gelesen hast und damit einverstanden bist.

description_full:
Die Koordination der EDEKA-Strategie erfolgt in der Hamburger EDEKA-Zentrale. Sie steuert das nationale Warengeschäft ebenso wie die erfolgreiche Kampagne „Wir lieben Lebensmittel“ und gibt vielfältige Impulse zur Realisierung verbundübergreifender Ziele. Mit dem Tochterunternehmen Netto Marken-Discount setzt sie darüber hinaus erfolgreiche Akzente im Discountgeschäft.

EDEKA erzielte 2025 mit rund 10.900 Märkten und rund 417.500 Mitarbeitenden einen Umsatz von 77,3 Mrd. Euro. Mit rund 20.900 Auszubildenden ist EDEKA einer der führenden Ausbilder in Deutschland.




Business Analyst bei EDEKA – Deine Chance, den Lebensmittelhandel neu zu denken

Als Business Analyst  im digitalen Marketing bei EDEKA stehst du im Herzen unserer digitalen Transformation. Du arbeitest an Themen, die Millionen Kund:innen täglich berühren – von der App‑Experience bis zu datengetriebenen Loyalty‑Mechaniken. Dein Impact ist unmittelbar sichtbar: Du identifizierst Chancen, entwickelst Hypothesen, bringst Innovationen voran und gestaltest aktiv die Zukunft des Einkaufens.

Bei uns hast du die Möglichkeit, anspruchsvolle Herausforderungen mit analytischem Scharfsinn, Kreativität und Ownership anzugehen. Dein Weg ist nicht vorgegeben – du formst ihn selbst, unterstützt von starken Teams aus CRM, App, IT und Marketing.



Deine Aufgaben



Kunden verstehen – Erlebnisse gestalten

Du analysierst Transaktions‑, Response‑ und Interaktionsdaten, um tiefgreifende Erkenntnisse über Kundenverhalten, Angebotswirkung und Aktivierungsmechaniken zu gewinnen.
Du entwickelst unsere digitalen Kundeninteraktionen – insbesondere in der EDEKA App – sowie Loyalty‑Programme kontinuierlich weiter.

Hypothesen testen – Wirkung maximieren

Du entwickelst, testest und validierst Hypothesen, um datenbasierte, umsetzbare und skalierbare Maßnahmen abzuleiten.
Du konzipierst, führst durch und analysierst A/B‑ und multivariate Tests, um Personalisierung, CRM und Angebotskommunikation messbar zu verbessern.

Innovation treiben – Lösungen in die Realität bringen

Du gestaltest datenbasierte Lösungen, die Kundenbindung stärken, Angebotsrelevanz erhöhen und das Nutzererlebnis spürbar verbessern.
Du identifizierst Optimierungs‑ und Innovationspotenziale entlang der gesamten Customer Journey – digital wie analog.

End‑to‑End‑Ownership übernehmen

Du arbeitest ganzheitlich: von der Analyse über die Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung mit den relevanten Fachbereichen.
Du übersetzt komplexe Sachverhalte in klare, präzise Entscheidungsgrundlagen – für operative Teams ebenso wie für strategische Stakeholder.


Dein Profil



Fachliche Skills & Erfahrung

Erfahrung mit Performance‑Daten, E‑Commerce, Datenmanagement oder Commercial Analytics.
Solide Kenntnisse in SQL, Excel, Power BI und Python.
Technologieverständnis zu digitalen Architekturen, Softwareentwicklung und datengetriebenen Produkten.
Sicherer Umgang mit Tools wie Jira, Confluence oder GitHub.
Ein Studium in (Wirtschafts‑)Informatik, Data Science, Digital Business, Mathematik, E‑Commerce, Wirtschaftspsychologie o. Ä.
Berufserfahrung im Handel, Start‑up oder Tech‑Umfeld – idealerweise mehrjährig.

Arbeitsweise & Denkstil

Du denkst analytisch, strukturiert und liebst es, komplexe Zusammenhänge zu durchdringen.
Du arbeitest ergebnisorientiert, pragmatisch und mit hohem Qualitätsanspruch.
Du kannst klare Hypothesen formulieren, Entscheidungen ableiten und Empfehlungen geben.
Du übernimmst Ownership und findest Wege statt Hürden.

Kommunikation & Zusammenarbeit

Du arbeitest gern in interdisziplinären Teams und vermittelst souverän zwischen Business & IT.
Du kannst datengetriebene Insights präzise und verständlich präsentieren.
Du bringst Engagement, Eigeninitiative und Verantwortungsbewusstsein mit.
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch‑ und Englischkenntnisse.


Unser Angebot


Ein sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem sehr erfolgreichen Handelsunternehmen mit Sitz in Hamburg, City Nord
Wir bieten flexible Arbeitszeiten, die Möglichkeit zum anteiligen mobilen Arbeiten und 30 Tage Urlaub
Individuelle Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten und Karrierechancen sind durch unsere internen Entwicklungsprogramme gewährleistet
Es erwartet dich täglich ein frisch gekochtes Mittagessen in unserer Kantine zu attraktiven Preisen
Ein leistungsgerechtes Vergütungspaket und vielfältige weitere Benefits, wie z.B. Fitnessstudio, Concierge-Service, Zuschuss zum Deutschlandticket, Dienstfahrrad, Vorteile bei zahlreichen Partnerunternehmen oder betriebliche Altersvorsorge sind für uns selbstverständlich
Daneben bieten wir auch ein umfangreiches und prämiertes Gesundheitsmanagement


Interessiert?



Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine Online-Bewerbung mit aussagekräftigen Bewerbungsunterlagen (Lebenslauf und vollständige Zeugnisse) unter Angabe Deiner Gehaltsvorstellung und Deines möglichen Eintrittstermins.

Senior Analyst Customer Data & Marketing Technology (m/w/d)

in Vollzeit (38-Stunden-Woche).

Die Rolle verantwortet die strategische Weiterentwicklung der MarCom-System- und Datenlandschaft, um eine konsistente und skalierbare Basis für datengetriebenes Marketing zu schaffen. Sie verbindet Systemarchitektur, Daten und Marketing-Use-Cases und überführt gewachsene Strukturen in eine integrierte Gesamtlogik.

Ziel ist die aktive Nutzung von Kundendaten zur Verbesserung von Personalisierung, Marketingautomatisierung und Nutzererlebnis über alle Touchpoints hinweg – inklusive hands-on Umsetzung von Projekten von der Idee bis zum Go-Live.

Was wir zusammen vorhaben


Sie entwickeln und verantworten die Customer-Data- und MarTech-Roadmap und richten diese konsequent an den strategischen Marketingzielen aus
Sie identifizieren, bewerten und priorisieren Use Cases über Teams und Systeme hinweg und etablieren dafür einheitliche Standards und Best Practices

About us

bolttech is a fast-growing global insurtech with a mission to build the world’s leading, technology-enabled ecosystem for protection and insurance. Our vision is to connect people with more ways to protect the things they value.




Launched in 2020 in Singapore, bolttech’s 1,600+ team serve customers in 35+ markets across Asia, Europe, North America, and Africa. With international licenses, an extensive partner network, and leading-edge technology and product innovation capabilities, our ecosystem brings together insurers and distributors to make it easier for customers to get the right protection at the point of need.




In this position you will…

Own the suite of digital tools that enable our European business partners to sell insurance and protection products to their end customers from discovery to launch and ongoing optimizations. Europe is our fastest growing market, and a strategic market for bolttech’s future success.




The role includes liaising with business stakeholders across the organization (business development, marketing, ops, tech, design) through agile ways of working to deliver world class digital solutions that drive innovation in the business.




You will be responsible to…

Setting the vision for your European product area – and drive that vision into a reality through collaboration with stakeholders and your agile squad(s)
Creating the business cases and maintain the product backlog according to business value and viability. Involves prioritizing stories, epics and themes to ensure work focuses on those with highest value that are aligned with product vision and company strategy.
Utilizing your deep knowledge of users and technology to define end-to-end requirements/stories that are comprehensible to developers/QA.
Working with designers (and other stakeholders) to ensure customers desire your product. This will involve testing with end users through prototypes, surveys, user sessions, etc.
Defining and execute go-to-market plans, working to ensure that product management, marketing, operations and sales have what they need to be successful.
Developing and maintain appropriate tracking and reporting of product performance post-launch to evaluate future iterations.




For you to be successful you are…



Impactful

Focused on objectives and identifies activities and key results required to meet them
Takes accountability and self-motivated to deliver results even in situations which are not straight-forward



Communication

Communication is clear and concise and uses data relevant to the audience to support points of view and suggestions
Adept communicator, listens to and integrates diverse contributions; explains issues clearly and succinctly



Adaptable

Manages multiple requirements in a positive and proactive manner adjusting approach to reach goals as required. Manages adversity well and adapts.
Is quick to adapt approach and act flexibly in response to changes in the strategic and/or operational direction of the organization



Collaborative

Partners with a range of people to create trust, and co-create and deliver mutually beneficial outcomes
Develops collaborative and dynamic working relationships to achieve the best possible outcomes




You will require the following qualifications and skills



Mandatory

Excellent Spanish and English language abilities
An experienced digital professional with 5+ years in the digital product space
Had the role Product Analyst, Product Owner or Product Manager for 2+ years
Proved your ability to articulate to business stakeholders why features are required, what problems they will solve and how they will benefit the end user
Experienced at writing end to end requirements/stories that are clear to software developers
Effective at collaborating with a global team, across multiple time-zones




Desired

Have experience working in the insurance (or insuretech) industry
Undergraduate degree in Business or Computing or Engineering
Experience working in a B2B2C organization




Diversity and Inclusion

At bolttech, we are diverse and inclusive. We value each person's unique skills, background, and identity. We never discriminate on cultural background, religion, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital or family status or any legally protected status. Everyone belongs. And because everyone’s different, we’re also flexible in the ways we work, so each person can bring their best efforts every day.

description_full:
Join Scrive and be part of an international, fast-growing company headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden. Collaborate with smart, caring, and driven colleagues across Europe as we shape the Scrive story. 🚀




The opportunity

We are currently looking for a Product Manager to join our team and play a key role in shaping the future of digital identity across Europe. At Scrive we offer a vibrant culture, opportunities for growth, and the chance to make a real impact in the rapidly evolving eSignature industry.




This is a uniquely broad product role — one that will grow not just your product management craft, but also your business acumen. You'll sharpen your technical and project management skills, develop the communication chops to navigate difficult business decisions, and help shape the strategies behind them.




This is an exciting opportunity to drive the growth and evolution of our Identity product portfolio (the enablement part of our portfolio) and build products that our customers love and trust.




📍 The role is ideally based in any of Scrive’s hubs (Stockholm, Amsterdam, Oslo, Berlin, or Copenhagen), but are also open for remote work. You will report to the Group Product Manager for Identity and Trust who is based in Stockholm.




As a Product Manager, you will:

Own the product vision and strategic roadmapping: shape the roadmap for your product area, balancing short-term delivery with long-term strategy, while ensuring alignment with customer needs and market trends.
Drive product outcomes and delivery: define success metrics for functionalities and collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver impactful, scalable solutions.
Collaborate in a product trio: work closely with Engineering, other Product Managers, and Product Design to ensure seamless delivery and adoption, while acting as a customer champion by gathering user feedback and competitor insights.
Lead commercial strategy and external partnerships: develop compelling pricing and Go-To-Market strategies to unlock growth, while building strong relationships with identity providers, integration partners, and key customers.




What we’re looking for

To succeed in this role, you should have:

2-4 years of experience in a Product Manager role, with a track record of leading complex SaaS products or services.
Proven track record in product development, roadmap planning, and translating customer needs into actionable features - preferably within a regulated or high-compliance industry.
Problem-solving skills: ability to navigate ambiguity with a proactive and scalable approach.
Excellent communication skills: strong relationship-building, collaboration, and storytelling abilities, with fluency in English.




🌟 Bonus points if you have:

Knowledge or experience working with electronic signatures and digital identity.
Experience in scaling products within a high-growth SaaS environment.
A background in building or scaling API-driven products where developers and technical teams are key users.
The ability to work from our offices in Stockholm, Copenhagen, Amsterdam or Berlin.




In this role, we see that you demonstrate strong skills in Discovery, Stakeholder communication, User empathy and Execution. These skills will play a key role in our assessment and discussions during the recruitment process.




Don’t meet every single requirement? Apply anyway! Studies show that women and people from underrepresented groups are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every qualification. At Scrive, we value diverse perspectives and encourage anyone who is excited about this role to apply.

description_full:
Welcome to Hacon, a leading IT company in the field of digital mobility solutions. As an independently managed company, we are part of Siemens Mobility. Whenever you are traveling with public transportation in Europe, you have already encountered us and our work. In our headquarter in Hannover, Berlin, and other locations, we develop software with over 500 employees that helps transportation providers worldwide make travel as easy as possible for their passengers. We love the idea that our solutions help establish public transportation as a genuine and environmentally friendly alternative to owning a car.

Whether by train, bus, bicycle, or e-scooter: We make mobility easy. Our software solutions support transportation companies in ensuring that travelers have a comfortable and seamless journey – from trip planning to reservations, mobility apps, mobile ticketing, to comprehensive MaaS (Mobility as a Service) solutions and on-demand offerings. Your work has an immediate impact on millions of people and contributes to the creation of environmentally friendly, efficient, and connected mobility.

Join us and shape the future of mobility with us!

We are looking for a qualified and motivated Product Manager (d/f/m) to join our development team. The responsibilities for this role include, but are not limited to:

What You'll Do


Navigate product transformation with resilience – providing stability and guidance to teams as the organization evolves toward product-centric ways of working
Together with the product roles in HAFAS.engine, define and maintain the long-term vision and roadmap, focusing on customer and user value, product viability, and strategic differentiation
Own feature-level strategy, development, and lifecycle management – from conception to market success
Conduct market and user research to identify needs, trends, and opportunities through desk research, interviews, workshops, and other validation methods in collaboration with user research roles
Transform customer problems and market opportunities into concrete product features – defining what needs to be built, why it matters, and how it delivers value across many customers
Take full ownership of feature validation, definition, pricing, and revenue generation – your success is measured by customer adoption and business impact
Validate requirements from tenders and customer inquiries together with expert roles in HAFAS.engine and overarching solution engineer roles
Cooperate with sales/bid teams to develop business models, pricing strategies, and product positioning
Represent the product in client-facing interactions through compelling presentations that inspire customers
Attend conferences and industry events to explore market trends and nurture customer relationships


What You'll Bring


Relevant university degree in Mobility & Transport, Engineering, Business, Computer Science or a related field
Minimum 3 years of professional experience in product management or adjacent roles, ideally in a technology-driven, agile environment
Experience in the information technology
Deep understanding of product development cycles, roadmap creation, and product lifecycle management
Experience with financial validation of features and P&L planning for organisational unit, ideally with B2B software pricing models
Strong analytical and strategic thinking abilities with a results-oriented approach
Understanding of the perspectives of various stakeholders in the mobility ecosystem, such as regulators, local authorities, transport operators and software providers
Passion for intermodal mobility – understanding how travelers combine different transport modes (train, bus, bike-sharing, ride-hailing) and how to provide seamless, integrated journey planning across these modes
Excellent communication and presentation skills to align stakeholders, inspire teams, and convince customers
Excellent English skills required; German skills are advantageous
Nice to have: Experience with SaaS pricing models


What We Offer

At our Hannover location, we offer you, among other things:


Family-friendly culture: Hybrid work, flexible working hours, 30 days of vacation, childcare subsidy and open-door policy
Stay mobile throughout the day: nationwide travel pass in Germany (Deutschland-Ticket) and bike leasing (Jobrad)
Stay active and relaxed: Gym & access to medical massages
Upskilling and professional development: Internal knowledge sharing & Siemens My Learning World
Financial benefits: Siemens share matching program
Healthy and delicious during the day: Bahlsen canteen & snack room
Celebrate and build teams together: Summer & christmas parties, team events like "Hacon on Tour" & "Hacon on Top
Office Location: Centrally located on Lister Meile with a rooftop terrace where you can enjoy BBQs


Interested? If you would like some more information about us before apllying, check our Homepage: https://www.hacon.de/ Would you like to find out more about our employee benefits? We'll give you the Top reasons, why you should work for us! We look forward to receiving your complete application documents, including your salary expectations.

Hacon Ingenieurgesellschaft mbH attaches great importance to equal opportunities. We welcome applications from people with disabilities.

description_full:
Description

About CADIS GmbH

CADIS GmbH is an established German software company and the team behind cadis® TMS, a platform built to digitally manage and control complex logistics processes. Our software empowers logistics and transport companies to take control of their operations, reducing complexity, cutting costs, and delivering measurable results for their customers.

With a growing client base across the DACH region and a product with a proven track record, we’re well-positioned for the next phase of growth. That’s where you come in.

Your Role

As a Product Manager, you are responsible for overseeing the development and lifecycle of one or more of our products. You will collaborate with engineering, marketing, design, sales, and other departments to define product vision, strategy, and roadmap – ensuring the delivery of high-quality products that meet market needs and align with our business objectives.

Key Responsibilities

Product Strategy & Vision


Define and communicate the product vision, strategy, and roadmap.
Conduct market research and customer interviews to identify product opportunities.
Analyze market trends, customer feedback, and competitive landscape to drive product decisions.
Develop AI-focused product strategies, including AI go-to-market approaches and pricing models for new products.


Product Development


Collaborate with engineering, design, and other teams to define product requirements and specifications.
Prioritize features, enhancements, and fixes based on business goals, customer needs, and technical feasibility.
Own and manage the product backlog, ensuring the team is focused on delivering the highest value items.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Work closely with engineering to ensure product requirements are understood and implemented.
Coordinate with sales, marketing, and customer support to ensure successful product launches and customer adoption.
Ensure clear communication of product goals, status, and timelines across teams.


Market & Customer Insights


Lead product discovery efforts, including customer research, competitor analysis, and usability testing.
Engage with customers and internal stakeholders to gather feedback and iterate on product features.
Track product performance and KPIs, adjusting strategy as needed based on insights and results.
Organize and lead regular customer advisory board sessions to gather strategic input, validate product direction, and strengthen key customer relationships.


Product Launch & Lifecycle Management


Lead the go-to-market strategy for new products and features, ensuring all teams are aligned for successful launches.
Define and execute AI-specific go-to-market strategies, including pricing model development for AI-powered products.
Monitor and optimize the product throughout its lifecycle, from concept to retirement.
Provide ongoing support for the product and address any issues that arise post-launch.


What You Bring


Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across teams.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills – able to think strategically and execute tactically.
Experience using product management tools (e.g., Jira, Trello, Aha!, Productboard).
Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple competing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency in both German and English (written and spoken) is required.
Willingness to travel – you’re comfortable visiting customers and being on-site when needed. (20%-30%)


What We Offer


Clear career path: a defined development trajectory with room to grow.
Direct collaboration with leadership: short decision-making cycles and flat hierarchies mean your ideas are heard and acted on.
Modern tech stack: cutting-edge AI tools built into our day-to-day workflows.
Flexible working: remote-first with hybrid options, designed around your life – not just your work hours.
Competitive compensation: attractive base salary with performance-based incentives.

At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

The Search at eBay team is responsible for the search page that drives a large part of eBay’s core marketplace business. The Search team strives to build an experience that enables users to easily explore, find, and buy what they want at the best value through an engaging, frictionless, and trusted Search experience, helping them make confident purchase decisions.

In this role, you will be responsible to building high quality advertising solutions for Search. Your expertise will drive the development of AI-driven features that improve product discovery, engagement, and overall ad quality. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AI/ML technologies, along with a passion for applying these skills to redefine the way customers interact with our platform.

What You Will Accomplish


Lead the product strategy and roadmap for improving advertising quality on search
Be a strategic problem solver and able to synthesize data from multiple sources (including analytics, user research, marketing research, and design) to identify key customer unmet needs, key solutions that can address the needs and build product vision with search item tiles
You will work closely with a cross-functional team of engineering, design, analytics, product marketing, and user research to deliver these products
You will lead the execution of your roadmap by working with data science, engineering, and analytics teams
Build MVPs, iterate fast and be the champion of execution excellence
Conduct AB tests for launch candidates and work with analytics teams to measure the impact on success metrics

At Sobi, each person brings their unique talents to work as a team and make a difference. We are dedicated to developing and delivering innovative therapies to improve the lives of people who live with a rare disease. Our edge comes from our team of people and our commitment to patients.

Here At Sobi, Our Mission And Culture Get Us Excited To Come To Work Every Day, But Here Are a Few More Reasons To Join Our Team


Competitive compensation for your work
Emphasis on work/life balance
Collaborative and team-oriented environment
Opportunities for professional growth
Diversity and Inclusion
Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments


Job Description

Objective for the company:

Develop the presence and recognition of Sobi as a credible partner within the community through supporting the development of relationships with key stakeholders, as well as the successful development and delivery of agreed pre and post license marketing projects.

Key Responsibilities


Development and execution of a country brand plan, including any required local strategy or tactical variations and budget (in alignment with the global brand plan)
Internal contact for Sobi HQ or global marketing team to align our local interests/needs with global activities.
Responsible for the strategical product management and the internal alignment with all other functions
Take over all communication responsibility for the product in charge
Analyze the success of current strategy and validate adaptations throughout the year
Support training of all internal and external staff
Drive branded (and non-branded) tactics that are in line with core product messaging and highest standards of compliance, e.g., branded commercial material, internet & social media
Develop strategic communications and/or training materials for internal and external customers on current projects and tactics and working with medical colleagues ensure the highest level of medical-scientific credibility in all Sobi external communications.
Work closely with local medical and patient access colleagues in Sobi, collaborate to define and deliver the overall marketing strategy, ensuring local customer needs and market requirements are included in the development of Sobi global strategy.
Responsible to execute defined key projects in line with local strategy to further strengthen Sobi’s position in the German market
Creation of monthly sales Comments, checking and monitoring the sales trend and costs budget; ensuring both forecast accuracy and keeping spending


Qualifications


University degree level education
Product management experience for a minimum of 3 years
Direct customer relationship management experience and an ability to engage with customer needs in delivering company projects
Proven ability to work in matrix organizations
Recent pre-launch or launch experience
Demonstrated ability to work independently and a “hands on” practical delivery of marketing projects


Additional Information

All Sobi employees need to demonstrate behaviors in line with Sobi's core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership. Are you ready to be on the Sobi team? Come join a culture that empowers every person to be the person that makes a difference for rare disease.

Why Join Us?

We are a global company with over 1,900 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. With a deeply skilled management team directing our day-to-day wins, and a Board with a stellar track record, we’re ready to take on the world’s diseases, ailments and adversity. Our people believe they have the power to make a positive impact in others’ lives because that’s exactly what we do here. If you’re seeking a career that taps into your talents in a way that makes the world a better, healthier place, we just may have a job for you.

We know our employees are our most valuable asset, and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.

Sobi Culture

At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases, and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.

As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open up more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.

An Equal Opportunity Employer
